190  // Rebuilds the container using cached layers by default or from scratch if full is true
191  func (i *invoker) RebuildContainer(ctx context.Context, full bool) error {
192  	ctx = i.appendMetadata(ctx)
193  	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, requestTimeout)
194  	defer cancel()
195  
196  	// If full is true, we want to pass false to the RPC call to indicate that we want to do a full rebuild
197  	incremental := !full
198  	response, err := i.codespaceClient.RebuildContainerAsync(ctx, &codespace.RebuildContainerRequest{Incremental: &incremental})
199  	if err != nil {
200  		return fmt.Errorf("failed to invoke rebuild RPC: %w", err)
201  	}
202  
203  	if !response.RebuildContainer {
204  		return fmt.Errorf("couldn't rebuild codespace")
205  	}
206  
207  	return nil
208  }
209  
210  // Starts a remote SSH server to allow the user to connect to the codespace via SSH
211  func (i *invoker) StartSSHServer(ctx context.Context) (int, string, error) {
212  	return i.StartSSHServerWithOptions(ctx, StartSSHServerOptions{})
213  }
214  
215  // Starts a remote SSH server to allow the user to connect to the codespace via SSH
216  func (i *invoker) StartSSHServerWithOptions(ctx context.Context, options StartSSHServerOptions) (int, string, error) {
217  	ctx = i.appendMetadata(ctx)
218  	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, requestTimeout)
219  	defer cancel()
220  
221  	userPublicKey := ""
222  	if options.UserPublicKeyFile != "" {
223  		publicKeyBytes, err := os.ReadFile(options.UserPublicKeyFile)
224  		if err != nil {
225  			return 0, "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read public key file: %w", err)
226  		}
227  
228  		userPublicKey = strings.TrimSpace(string(publicKeyBytes))
229  	}
230  
231  	response, err := i.sshClient.StartRemoteServerAsync(ctx, &ssh.StartRemoteServerRequest{UserPublicKey: userPublicKey})
232  	if err != nil {
233  		return 0, "", fmt.Errorf("failed to invoke SSH RPC: %w", err)
234  	}
235  
236  	if !response.Result {
237  		return 0, "", fmt.Errorf("failed to start SSH server: %s", response.Message)
238  	}
239  
240  	port, err := strconv.Atoi(response.ServerPort)
241  	if err != nil {
242  		return 0, "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse SSH server port: %w", err)
243  	}
244  
245  	if !isUsernameValid(response.User) {
246  		return 0, "", fmt.Errorf("invalid username: %s", response.User)
247  	}
248  	return port, response.User, nil
249  }
250  
251  func listenTCP() (*net.TCPListener, error) {
252  	// We will end up using this same address to connect, so specify the IP also or the connect will fail
253  	addr, err := net.ResolveTCPAddr("tcp", "127.0.0.1:0")
254  	if err != nil {
255  		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to build tcp address: %w", err)
256  	}
257  	listener, err := net.ListenTCP("tcp", addr)
258  	if err != nil {
259  		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to listen to local port over tcp: %w", err)
260  	}
261  
262  	return listener, nil
263  }
264  
265  // KeepAlive sets a flag to continuously send activity signals to
266  // the codespace even if there is no other activity (e.g. stdio)
267  func (i *invoker) KeepAlive() {
268  	i.keepAliveOverride = true
269  }
270  
271  // Periodically check whether there is a reason to keep the connection alive, and if so, notify the codespace to do so
272  func (i *invoker) heartbeat(ctx context.Context, interval time.Duration) {
273  	ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
274  	defer ticker.Stop()
275  
276  	for {
277  		select {
278  		case <-ctx.Done():
279  			return
280  		case <-ticker.C:
281  			reason := ""
282  
283  			// If the keep alive override flag is set, we don't need to check for activity on the forwarder
284  			// Otherwise, grab the reason from the forwarder
285  			if i.keepAliveOverride {
286  				reason = keepAliveEventName
287  			} else {
288  				reason = i.fwd.GetKeepAliveReason()
289  			}
290  			_ = i.notifyCodespaceOfClientActivity(ctx, reason)
291  		}
292  	}
293  }
294  
295  func (i *invoker) notifyCodespaceOfClientActivity(ctx context.Context, activity string) error {
296  	ctx = i.appendMetadata(ctx)
297  	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, requestTimeout)

// gh-cli / internal/codespaces/api/api.go:831
831  	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 2*time.Minute)
832  	defer cancel()
833  
834  	ticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)
835  	defer ticker.Stop()
836  
837  	for {
838  		select {
839  		case <-ctx.Done():
840  			return nil, ctx.Err()
841  		case <-ticker.C:
842  			codespace, err = a.GetCodespace(ctx, codespace.Name, false)
843  			if err != nil {
844  				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get codespace: %w", err)
845  			}
846  
847  			// we continue to poll until the codespace shows as provisioned
848  			if codespace.State != CodespaceStateAvailable {
849  				continue
850  			}
851  

// gh-cli / pkg/cmdutil/factory.go:29-35
29  	// It would be nice if Config were just loaded once at startup and an error
30  	// were returned, but this would prevent commands like "gh version" from running.
31  	// So for now, we eagerly load the config and don't fail if there is an error,
32  	// and defer the error handling to commands that need it.
33  	// HOWEVER, as an additional point, the root command setup currently DOES call
34  	// this and errors, so we never get to "gh version" anyway.
35  	// We need to revisit that, but I don't want to make it worse.

// gh-cli / internal/codespaces/ssh.go:164
164  		if len(arg) == 2 && strings.Contains(unaryFlags, arg[1:2]) {
165  			if i++; i == len(args) {
166  				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("flag: %s requires an argument", arg)
167  			}
168  
169  			cmdArgs = append(cmdArgs, args[i])
170  		}

190  		if err != nil {
191  			return nil, err
192  		}
193  		opts := api.HTTPClientOptions{
194  			Config:            cfg.Authentication(),
195  			Log:               ios.ErrOut,
196  			LogColorize:       ios.ColorEnabled(),
197  			AppVersion:        appVersion,
198  			InvokingAgent:     invokingAgent,
199  			TelemetryDisabler: telemetryDisabler,
200  		}
201  		client, err := api.NewHTTPClient(opts)
202  		if err != nil {
203  			return nil, err
204  		}
205  		client.Transport = api.ExtractHeader("X-GitHub-SSO", &ssoHeader)(client.Transport)
206  		return client, nil
207  	}
208  }

211  	return func() (*http.Client, error) {

213  			Log:           ios.ErrOut,
214  			LogColorize:   ios.ColorEnabled(),
215  			AppVersion:    appVersion,
216  			InvokingAgent: invokingAgent,
217  			// This is required to prevent automatic setting of auth and other headers.
218  			SkipDefaultHeaders: true,
219  			TelemetryDisabler:  telemetryDisabler,
220  		}
221  		client, err := api.NewHTTPClient(opts)
222  		if err != nil {
223  			return nil, err
224  		}
225  		return client, nil
226  	}
227  }

418  		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, permissionsPollingTimeout)
419  		defer cancel()
420  
421  		done := make(chan error, 1)
422  		go func() {
423  			for {
424  				accepted, err := a.apiClient.GetCodespacesPermissionsCheck(ctx, createParams.RepositoryID, createParams.Branch, createParams.DevContainerPath)
425  				if err != nil {
426  					done <- err
427  					return
428  				}
429  
430  				if accepted {
431  					done <- nil
432  					return
433  				}
434  
435  				// Wait before polling again
436  				time.Sleep(permissionsPollingInterval)
437  			}
438  		}()
439  
440  		select {
441  		case err := <-done:
442  			return err
443  		case <-ctx.Done():
444  			return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for permissions to be accepted in the browser")
445  		}
446  	})

437  			}
438  		}()

441  		case err := <-done:
442  			return err
443  		case <-ctx.Done():
444  			return fmt.Errorf("timed out waiting for permissions to be accepted in the browser")
445  		}
446  	})
447  }

# Concurrency & Async Patterns - Combined Study Report

## Study Parameters

| Field | Value |

| 2 | chezmoi | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/chezmoi` | 08-concurrency |
| 3 | dive | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/dive` | 08-concurrency |
| 4 | fzf | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/fzf` | 08-concurrency |
| 5 | gdu | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/gdu` | 08-concurrency |
| 6 | gh-cli | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/gh-cli` | gh-cli |
| 7 | go-task | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/go-task` | go-task |
| 8 | helm | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/helm` | 08-concurrency |
| 9 | k9s | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/k9s` | 08-concurrency |

| 14 | restic | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/restic` | 08-concurrency |
| 15 | urfave-cli | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/urfave-cli` | urfave-cli |
| 16 | yq | `/home/antonioborgerees/coding/go-cli-study/repos/yq` | 08-concurrency |

## Executive Summary

Concurrency approaches across elite Go CLI projects fall into three broad clusters:

1. **Structured concurrency (7–8/10)**: Projects like fzf, gdu, lazygit, go-task, restic, and opencode use `errgroup.Group`, `sync.WaitGroup`, channels, and mutexes to coordinate goroutines with clear lifecycle boundaries.

2. **Functional but scattered (5–6/10)**: Projects like age, chezmoi, dive, helm, k9s, rclone, mitchellh-cli use concurrency but without consistent patterns — goroutines launch ad-hoc, cleanup is implicit, and coordination is manual.

3. **No concurrency (3/10)**: Projects like yq and urfave-cli are intentionally single-threaded by design, processing data sequentially without goroutines.

The main finding: most Go CLI projects that do concurrency use `sync.WaitGroup` as their primary coordination primitive. `golang.org/x/sync/errgroup` is used by about half of them (lazygit, restic, go-task, rclone, gh-cli) but not uniformly. Context cancellation is the dominant cleanup mechanism; explicit wait-with-timeout patterns are rare but appear in higher-rated projects.

## Core Thesis

Go CLI projects tend toward "just enough concurrency" — they use concurrency where I/O patterns demand it (parallel API calls, filesystem walking, background tasks) but avoid general-purpose parallel processing. Projects with high ratings share common traits: localized goroutine launch sites, structured coordination primitives (errgroup or WaitGroup), mutex protection for shared state, and context-driven cleanup. Projects with low ratings either lack concurrency entirely or use it inconsistently without clear lifecycle management.

## Rating Summary

| Repo | Score | Approach | Main Strength | Main Concern |
|------|-------|----------|---------------|--------------|
| urfave-cli | 10 | Single-threaded | Zero concurrency complexity | Cannot parallelize |
| fzf | 8 | Event-driven loop | Central EventBox, structured shutdown | Terminal loop complexity |
| gdu | 8 | Semaphore + WaitGroup | Custom WaitGroup, bounded parallelism | Global semaphore, complex mutex |
| lazygit | 8 | errgroup + stop channels | Structured patterns, panic recovery | ViewBufferManager complexity |
| restic | 8 | errgroup + worker pools | Consistent patterns, clean shutdown | Nested errgroup error attribution |
| go-task | 8 | errgroup + semaphore | Failfast context, deduplication | Watch mode goroutine lifecycle |
| opencode | 8 | WaitGroup + context | Explicit cleanup with timeout | No errgroup, ad-hoc error collection |
| gh-cli | 7 | errgroup + WaitGroup | Parallel API fan-out | Scattered goroutine launching |
| helm | 7 | WaitGroup + channels | Localized patterns | No errgroup, manual cleanup |
| k9s | 7 | WorkerPool + channels | Semaphore limiting | No errgroup, mutex-heavy |
| rclone | 7 | errgroup + WaitGroup | Multiple patterns | Scattered goroutine spawning |
| chezmoi | 6 | errgroup + mutex | Structured dir walking | Watch goroutine lacks shutdown |
| dive | 6 | Channel generators | Clean channel pattern | No errgroup, fire-and-forget |
| mitchellh-cli | 6 | Single goroutine | Clean select pattern | No parallel execution |
| age | 3 | Sequential + atomic cache | Plugin subprocess isolation | No concurrency design |
| yq | 3 | Sequential loop | Simple, no races | No parallelism |

## Approach Models

### Model 1: Structured Fan-out with errgroup

Used by: lazygit, restic, go-task, rclone, gh-cli

This model uses `golang.org/x/sync/errgroup.Group` with `errgroup.WithContext(ctx)` to launch parallel operations. Error collection and cancellation propagation are automatic. Goroutines are typically spawned at a single coordination point (e.g., `errgroup.Go(func() error {...})`), and `g.Wait()` blocks until all complete.

Key pattern:
```go
g, ctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)
for _, item := range items {
    g.Go(func() error {
        return process(item)
    })
}
return g.Wait()
```

Evidence: `lazygit/pkg/gui/controllers/helpers/fixup_helper.go:267`, `restic/internal/repository/repository.go:567`, `go-task/task.go:87`

### Model 2: WaitGroup + Semaphore

Used by: gdu, k9s, helm

This model uses `sync.WaitGroup` for lifecycle tracking combined with a semaphore channel (buffered `chan struct{}`) to limit concurrent goroutines. Clean for bounded parallelism but manual error handling.

Key pattern:
```go
sem := make(chan struct{}, maxConcurrency)
var wg sync.WaitGroup
for _, item := range items {
    sem <- struct{}{}  // acquire
    wg.Add(1)
    go func() {
        defer wg.Done()
        defer func() { <-sem }()  // release
        process(item)
    }()
}
wg.Wait()
```

Evidence: `gdu/pkg/analyze/parallel.go:13`, `k9s/internal/pool.go:30`

### Model 3: EventBox / Channel-based Coordination

Used by: fzf, opencode

This model uses a central event dispatcher (typically `sync.Cond`-based EventBox in fzf, channels in opencode) to coordinate between subsystems. Goroutines communicate via message passing rather than shared memory.

Key pattern (fzf):
```go
type EventBox struct {
    cond sync.Cond
    mut sync.Mutex
    events map[EventType][]EventHandler
}
```

Evidence: `fzf/src/util/eventbox.go:27`, `opencode/internal/pubsub/broker.go:10`

### Model 4: Single Goroutine / Sequential

Used by: mitchellh-cli, urfave-cli

Minimal concurrency: one goroutine for input handling (mitchellh-cli) or none at all (urfave-cli). All work is sequential. No coordination primitives needed.

Evidence: `mitchellh-cli/ui.go:76`, `urfave-cli/command_run.go:92`

### Model 5: No Concurrency

Used by: yq, age

Sequential file processing with no parallelism. age uses a single atomic cache; yq uses simple for-loops.

Evidence: `yq/pkg/yqlib/stream_evaluator.go:52`, `age/internal/stream/stream.go:354`

## Pattern Catalog

### Pattern: errgroup.WithContext for Parallel Fan-out

**What**: Launch N goroutines via `errgroup.Group.Go()`, collect errors, propagate cancellation.

**Repos**: lazygit (`fixup_helper.go:267`), restic (`repository.go:567`), go-task (`task.go:87`), rclone (`operations.go:76`)

**Why it works**: Automatic error collection and context cancellation when any goroutine fails. Clean `g.Wait()` interface.

**When to copy**: When launching parallel I/O operations (API calls, file processing) that need to fail fast on error.

**When overkill**: For simple fire-and-forget operations where errors don't need to be collected.

### Pattern: Semaphore via Buffered Channel

**What**: `make(chan struct{}, N)` to limit concurrent goroutines.

**Repos**: gdu (`parallel.go:13`), k9s (`pool.go:30`), go-task (`concurrency.go:3-22`)

**Why it works**: Simple, idiomatic Go pattern. Acquire before work, release after.

**When to copy**: When you need bounded parallelism without the overhead of a worker pool.

**When risky**: If acquire/release isn't strictly matched (e.g., early return without release), goroutines leak.

### Pattern: Stop Channel for Graceful Shutdown

**What**: `<-stopChan struct{}` in select statements to signal goroutine exit.

**Repos**: lazygit (`gui.go:89`), opencode (`broker.go:67`), dive (`resolver.go:53`)

**Why it works**: Channels close cleanly, range over closed channel yields zero value.

**When to copy**: For long-lived goroutines that need to respond to shutdown signals.

**When overkill**: For short-lived goroutines that exit via other mechanisms.

### Pattern: Custom WaitGroup

**What**: A WaitGroup where `Add()` can be called after goroutine spawn.

**Repos**: gdu (`wait.go:7-11`)

**Why it works**: Allows dynamic work discovery — goroutines can add more work mid-execution.

**When to copy**: When work items are discovered recursively (e.g., directory traversal).

**When risky**: More complex than stdlib WaitGroup; subtle locking behavior.

### Pattern: EventBox (sync.Cond-based)

**What**: Central event dispatcher using `sync.Cond` for blocking wait on events.

**Repos**: fzf (`eventbox.go:27-39`)

**Why it works**: Allows many goroutines to wait on events without busy-waiting.

**When to copy**: For UI frameworks with multiple concurrent event sources.

**When overkill**: For simple fan-out patterns, `errgroup` is simpler.

### Pattern: Deferred Cancel + Explicit Wait with Timeout

**What**: `defer cancel()` paired with `waitGroup.Wait()` with a timeout.

**Repos**: opencode (`root.go:261-279`), rclone (`server.go:581-598`)

**Why it works**: Prevents indefinite blocking while ensuring cleanup completes.

**When to copy**: For CLI shutdown sequences where goroutines may hang.

### Pattern: sync.Once for One-time Cleanup

**What**: `sync.Once` ensures cleanup callbacks run exactly once.

**Repos**: lazygit (`tasks.go:122`), rclone (`batcher.go:50`), opencode (`shell.go:44`)

**Why it works**: Thread-safe, no double-cleanup concerns.

**When to copy**: For shutdown hooks, lazy initializers.

### Pattern: Atomic Cache (Lock-free)

**What**: `atomic.Pointer[T]` for shared cache without mutex overhead.

**Repos**: age (`stream.go:354`), fzf (`atomicbool.go:16`)

**Why it works**: Lock-free reads for read-heavy data structures.

**When to copy**: For simple state that doesn't need full mutex protection.

**When risky**: Only one value at a time; not suitable for complex state.

## Key Differences

### errgroup vs WaitGroup

**errgroup** (lazygit, restic, go-task, rclone, gh-cli): Automatic error propagation and context cancellation. Cleaner for operations where any failure should cancel siblings.

**WaitGroup** (gdu, helm, k9s, opencode): Manual, no error propagation. Better when operations are independent and errors don't require cancellation.

**No structured coordination** (age, dive, yq): Goroutines fire-and-forget or use raw channels.

The difference is not quality — it's fit. `errgroup` shines for fan-out API calls; `WaitGroup` shines for independent parallel tasks.

### Centralized vs Scattered Goroutine Launch

**Localized** (fzf, gdu, restic, lazygit): Goroutines spawned from clear coordination points (`core.go`, `app.go`). Easier to audit.

**Scattered** (gh-cli, helm, rclone): Goroutines launched from many command files. Harder to reason about global lifecycle.

### Cleanup Models

**Context cancellation** (fzf, restic, opencode): `defer cancel()`, goroutines listen on `ctx.Done()`. Clean for hierarchical cancellation.

**Explicit channels** (lazygit, k9s): `stopChan` closed on shutdown. More explicit but requires channel ownership discipline.

**Defer + Wait** (helm, gdu): `defer wg.Wait()` blocks until all goroutines complete. Simple but no timeout.

**Implicit** (dive, age): No explicit cleanup — relies on goroutines completing or process exit. Risk of leaks.

### Race Prevention

**Mutex-heavy** (gdu, k9s, helm): `sync.Mutex` or `sync.RWMutex` protecting shared state.

**Atomic-first** (fzf, age): Use `atomic` operations for simple state; mutex only for complex state.

**Deadlock-detecting mutex** (lazygit): Uses `go-deadlock` package in debug mode to detect circular lock waits.

## Tradeoffs

### Simplicity vs Throughput

Projects like yq and urfave-cli choose sequential processing — no concurrency complexity, no races, no leaks. But they can't utilize multiple CPU cores. Projects like gdu and fzf choose parallel processing — higher throughput, more complexity.

**Rule**: If your CLI is I/O-bound (network, filesystem), concurrency helps. If it's CPU-bound, you need parallelization. If it's simple (parse → process → output), sequential may be fine.

### errgroup vs WaitGroup + Semaphore

`errgroup` provides automatic error propagation and cancellation propagation — valuable when operations should fail fast. But it doesn't limit concurrency by default (use `SetLimit()`).

`WaitGroup + semaphore` is more manual but gives explicit control over concurrency limits. Better for bounded parallelism where you don't want unlimited goroutines.

**Rule**: Use `errgroup` when operations are homogeneous and should fail fast. Use `WaitGroup + semaphore` when you need explicit concurrency control.

### Global State vs Dependency Injection

Some projects use global variables for coordination (dive's bus singleton, gdu's global `concurrencyLimit`). This is simple but makes testing harder.

Others use dependency injection (lazygit's `BackgroundRoutineMgr`, opencode's `Broker[T]`).

**Rule**: Global state is fine for CLIs where you have one instance. Dependency injection is better for testable code.

### Channel-based vs Mutex-based

Channels excel at communication (signals, results, stop requests). Mutexes excel at protecting shared state.

Most high-rated projects use both: channels for coordination, mutexes for state protection.

**Rule**: Use channels for ownership transfer and signals; use mutexes for protecting data.

## Decision Guide

**Q: Should I use concurrency at all?**

A: If your CLI needs to: run multiple commands in parallel, watch files, stream output, process large files with parallel I/O — yes. If it's a simple filter (read stdin, process, write stdout), sequential is fine.

**Q: errgroup or WaitGroup?**

A: Use `errgroup.WithContext(ctx)` when you need cancellation propagation and error collection across parallel operations. Use `WaitGroup` when operations are independent and you just need to wait for completion.

**Q: How do I limit concurrency?**

A: Semaphore pattern: `make(chan struct{}, N)`. Acquire before work, release after. Or `errgroup.SetLimit(N)`.

**Q: How should goroutines signal shutdown?**

A: `context.Context` with `defer cancel()` is the most idiomatic. Stop channels also work but require explicit close.

**Q: What about cleanup on exit?**

A: Pair `defer cancel()` with `waitGroup.Wait()` and add a timeout: `select { case <-waitGroup.Done(): ... case <-time.After(5 * time.Second): ... }`.

**Q: How do I prevent races?**

A: Protect shared state with `sync.Mutex` or `sync.RWMutex`. Use `sync/atomic` for simple counters and booleans. Use `sync.Once` for one-time initialization.

## Practical Tips

1. **Localize goroutine launch sites**: Create dedicated methods/functions for spawning goroutines rather than scattering `go func()` throughout. This makes lifecycle auditable.

2. **Use errgroup for fan-out I/O**: When fetching multiple resources in parallel (API calls, file reads), `errgroup.WithContext` provides clean error collection and cancellation.

3. **Use semaphore for bounded parallelism**: A buffered channel of struct{} is the idiomatic way to limit concurrent goroutines without a worker pool abstraction.

4. **Always pair defer cancel with context**: Every goroutine that receives a context should have `defer cancel()` to ensure cleanup on both success and failure paths.

5. **Use sync.Once for one-time initialization**: Avoids initialization races without explicit mutex locking on the init path.

6. **Use atomic for simple state**: `atomic.Int32`, `atomic.Pointer[T]` for lock-free reads/writes of simple state.

7. **Document lock ordering**: If you use multiple mutexes, document the lock ordering contract (e.g., "uiMutex must be locked before mutex").

8. **Add panic recovery wrappers**: Wrap goroutines that could panic with `defer func() { if r := recover(); r != nil { log.Error(r) } }()` to prevent crashes.

9. **Use timeout on wait operations**: Don't `waitGroup.Wait()` without a timeout — use `select` with a timeout to prevent indefinite hangs.

10. **Consider stop channels over context for lifecycle**: For graceful shutdown signaling, a `stop chan struct{}` that gets closed is sometimes clearer than context cancellation, especially when the same signal needs to reach many goroutines.

## Anti-Patterns / Caution Signs

1. **Fire-and-forget goroutines with no cleanup mechanism**: The notification goroutine in `dive/resolver.go:70` fires and is never waited on. If it runs after the function returns, it may log after the work is done.

2. **Global mutex contention**: gdu's package-level `concurrencyLimit` (`parallel.go:13`) means all ParallelAnalyzers share the same semaphore. One analyzer can starve others.

3. **No context cancellation for HTTP reads**: chezmoi's HTTP reader goroutine (`readhttpresponse.go:172`) has no way to abort a slow read — only checks `Canceled()` after `program.Run()` returns.

4. **Global bus singleton**: dive's partybus publisher is a global variable set at startup (`bus.go:5`). Common in CLIs but makes testing harder and introduces global mutable state.

5. **Unbounded goroutine spawning**: Some patterns launch a goroutine per item (e.g., `gh-cli/manager.go:198-203` launches one per extension). Doesn't scale to large lists.

6. **No timeout on WaitGroup.Wait()**: If a goroutine hangs, operations like `gh-cli/manager.go:205` hang indefinitely.

7. **Channel without buffer causing deadlock**: rclone's `batchPerform` error channel is unbuffered (`client.go:981`). If caller stops reading early, goroutines block on send.

8. **Race between GetFiles and AddFile**: gdu's `GetFiles` (non-locked) at `file.go:168-181` doesn't hold mutex, but `GetFilesLocked` does. Code calling `GetFiles` while another goroutine calls `AddFile` could race.

9. **Watch goroutine with no shutdown**: chezmoi's watch goroutine (`editcmd.go:232`) has no mechanism to terminate cleanly — only exits when `watcher.Close()` is called by defer.

10. **No errgroup means no automatic cancellation**: helm's `batchPerform` runs all goroutines to completion regardless of errors. No sibling cancellation.

## Notable Absences

- **No `golang.org/x/sync/errgroup`** in: age, chezmoi, dive, gdu, helm, k9s, mitchellh-cli, opencode, yq, urfave-cli
- **No `sync.WaitGroup`** in: age, yq, urfave-cli, mitchellh-cli (only single goroutine)
- **No `sync.Mutex`** in: yq, urfave-cli (single-threaded)
- **No `sync.Cond`** in most projects (only fzf's EventBox)
- **No `sync.Map`** found in most projects (only opencode, rclone, gdu's parallel_top_dir)
- **No `-race` in CI/testing**: No evidence of `go test -race` in most repos

## Per-Repo Notes

| Repo | Notable Observation |
|------|---------------------|
| age | Sequential encryption pipeline with atomic chunk cache. Single WaitTimer goroutine for plugin callbacks. No parallelization in main data path. |
| chezmoi | errgroup for directory walking, fsnotify watch goroutine, tea.Model for async UI. Watch goroutine lacks explicit shutdown mechanism. |
| dive | Channel generators for filetree indexing, fire-and-forget notification goroutine, gocui owns main loop. No structured coordination. |
| fzf | Central EventBox with sync.Cond, dual event loops (terminal + matcher), atomic booleans, explicit lock ordering contract. Most sophisticated concurrency in the set. |
| gdu | Custom WaitGroup enabling dynamic work discovery, semaphore-based parallelism limiting, RWMutex on Dir for read-heavy access. Global semaphore is a concern. |
| gh-cli | errgroup for fan-out API calls (10 workers), scattered goroutine launching across many commands. No universal shutdown framework. |
| go-task | errgroup with failfast context, semaphore for concurrency limiting, execution deduplication via hash map. Watch mode goroutines lack cleanup. |
| helm | WaitGroup for batch operations, channels for signals/results, global createMutex. No errgroup, no automatic cancellation propagation. |
| k9s | WorkerPool with semaphore, context cancellation, mutex-heavy design. BOZO comment in forwarder cleanup indicates uncertainty. |
| lazygit | errgroup for parallel git ops, deadlock-detecting mutex, BackgroundRoutineMgr, ViewBufferManager with 5 concurrent goroutines. Most structured patterns. |
| mitchellh-cli | Single goroutine for input, select-based rendezvous with signal handling. No parallel command execution. |
| opencode | WaitGroup for shutdown tracking, sync.Map for request cancellation, explicit cleanup with 5s timeout. No errgroup. |
| rclone | errgroup + WaitGroup + semaphore + sync.Cond for VFS cache. Multiple patterns, scattered spawning. |
| restic | Consistent errgroup usage, worker pools (TreeSaver, PackerUploader), future/promise pattern, semaphore for backend limiting. Cleanest pattern consistency. |
| urfave-cli | Purely sequential, no goroutines. Context for cancellation propagation only. Elegant simplicity. |
| yq | Sequential for-loop, no concurrency primitives. No context support. |

## Open Questions

1. **Why don't more projects use errgroup?** It's the standard structured concurrency primitive, but half the projects that do concurrency still use raw WaitGroup. Is it unfamiliarity, or a deliberate choice?

2. **When is a global semaphore a problem?** gdu's package-level `concurrencyLimit` is shared across all analyzers. In practice, how often do multiple analyzers run concurrently in a single CLI invocation?

3. **Is the deadlock-detecting mutex worth the overhead?** lazygit uses `go-deadlock` in debug mode. Does it catch real issues, or is it noise?

4. **Should CLI frameworks provide concurrency primitives?** urfave-cli is intentionally single-threaded. But users who need concurrency must manage it outside the framework. Should frameworks offer opt-in parallelism?

5. **How do projects handle goroutine leak detection?** rclone mentions visible goroutine leak fixes in changelog. Is there a systematic approach to detecting leaks, or only post-hoc bug reports?

// fzf / fzf/src/util/eventbox.go:27
27  func (b *EventBox) Wait(callback func(*Events)) {
28  	b.cond.L.Lock()
29  
30  	if len(b.events) == 0 {
31  		b.cond.Wait()
32  	}
33  
34  	callback(&b.events)
35  	b.cond.L.Unlock()
36  }
37  
38  // Set turns on the event type on the box
39  func (b *EventBox) Set(event EventType, value any) {
40  	b.cond.L.Lock()
41  	b.events[event] = value
42  	if _, found := b.ignore[event]; !found {
43  		b.cond.Broadcast()
44  	}
45  	b.cond.L.Unlock()
46  }

// go-task / concurrency.go:3-22
3  func (e *Executor) acquireConcurrencyLimit() func() {
4  	if e.concurrencySemaphore == nil {
5  		return emptyFunc
6  	}
7  
8  	e.concurrencySemaphore <- struct{}{}
9  	return func() {
10  		<-e.concurrencySemaphore
11  	}
12  }
13  
14  func (e *Executor) releaseConcurrencyLimit() func() {
15  	if e.concurrencySemaphore == nil {
16  		return emptyFunc
17  	}
18  
19  	<-e.concurrencySemaphore
20  	return func() {
21  		e.concurrencySemaphore <- struct{}{}
22  	}

// fzf / src/util/atomicbool.go:16
16  type AtomicBool struct {
17  	state int32 // "1" is true, "0" is false
18  }
19  
20  // NewAtomicBool returns a new AtomicBool
21  func NewAtomicBool(initialState bool) *AtomicBool {
22  	return &AtomicBool{state: convertBoolToInt32(initialState)}
23  }
24  
25  // Get returns the current boolean value synchronously
26  func (a *AtomicBool) Get() bool {
27  	return atomic.LoadInt32(&a.state) == 1
28  }
29  
30  // Set updates the boolean value synchronously
31  func (a *AtomicBool) Set(newState bool) bool {
32  	atomic.StoreInt32(&a.state, convertBoolToInt32(newState))
33  	return newState
34  }

188  func makeNightriderFrames(shape string, padding rune, width int) []string {
189  	delta := width - len(shape)
190  	if delta <= 0 {

293  func compareDirEntries(a, b fs.DirEntry) int {
294  	aName, bName := a.Name(), b.Name()
295  	if compare := cmp.Compare(sourceDirEntryOrder[aName], sourceDirEntryOrder[bName]); compare != 0 {
296  		return compare
297  	}
298  	return cmp.Compare(aName, bName)

167  // GetFiles returns all files in directory as a sorted iterator
168  func (f *Dir) GetFiles(sortBy fs.SortBy, order fs.SortOrder) iter.Seq[fs.Item] {
169  	return func(yield func(fs.Item) bool) {
170  		// Make a copy to avoid modifying the original slice
171  		sorted := make(fs.Files, len(f.Files))
172  		copy(sorted, f.Files)
173  		sortFiles(sorted, sortBy, order)
174  
175  		for _, item := range sorted {
176  			if !yield(item) {
177  				return
178  			}
179  		}
180  	}
181  }

183  // GetFilesLocked returns all files in directory as a sorted iterator
184  // It is safe to call this function from multiple goroutines
185  func (f *Dir) GetFilesLocked(sortBy fs.SortBy, order fs.SortOrder) iter.Seq[fs.Item] {
186  	return func(yield func(fs.Item) bool) {
187  		f.m.RLock()
188  		defer f.m.RUnlock()
189  
190  		// Make a copy to avoid modifying the original slice
191  		sorted := make(fs.Files, len(f.Files))
192  		copy(sorted, f.Files)
193  		sortFiles(sorted, sortBy, order)
194  
195  		for _, item := range sorted {
196  			if !yield(item) {
197  				return
198  			}
199  		}
200  	}

97  			}

106  	}

108  	return nil

111  func (e *Executor) splitRegularAndWatchCalls(calls ...*Call) (regularCalls []*Call, watchCalls []*Call, err error) {
112  	for _, c := range calls {
113  		t, err := e.GetTask(c)
114  		if err != nil {
115  			return nil, nil, err
116  		}
117  
118  		if e.Watch || t.Watch {
119  			watchCalls = append(watchCalls, c)
120  		} else {
121  			regularCalls = append(regularCalls, c)
122  		}
123  	}
124  	return regularCalls, watchCalls, err
125  }
126  
127  // RunTask runs a task by its name
128  func (e *Executor) RunTask(ctx context.Context, call *Call) error {
129  	// Inject prompted vars into call if available
130  	if e.promptedVars != nil {
131  		if call.Vars == nil {
132  			call.Vars = ast.NewVars()
133  		}
134  		for name, v := range e.promptedVars.All() {
135  			// Only inject if not already set in call
136  			if _, ok := call.Vars.Get(name); !ok {
137  				call.Vars.Set(name, v)
138  			}
139  		}
140  	}
141  
142  	t, err := e.FastCompiledTask(call)
143  	if err != nil {
144  		return err
145  	}
146  	if !shouldRunOnCurrentPlatform(t.Platforms) {
147  		e.Logger.VerboseOutf(logger.Yellow, `task: %q not for current platform - ignored\n`, call.Task)
148  		return nil
149  	}
150  
151  	// Check required vars early (before template compilation) if we can't prompt.
152  	// This gives a clear "missing required variables" error instead of a template error.
153  	if !e.canPrompt() {
154  		if err := e.areTaskRequiredVarsSet(t); err != nil {
155  			return err
156  		}
157  	}
158  
159  	t, err = e.CompiledTask(call)
160  	if err != nil {
161  		return err
162  	}
163  
164  	// Check if condition after CompiledTask so dynamic variables are resolved
165  	if strings.TrimSpace(t.If) != "" {
166  		if err := execext.RunCommand(ctx, &execext.RunCommandOptions{
167  			Command: t.If,
168  			Dir:     t.Dir,
169  			Env:     env.Get(t),
170  		}); err != nil {
171  			e.Logger.VerboseOutf(logger.Yellow, "task: if condition not met - skipped: %q\n", call.Task)
172  			return nil
173  		}
174  	}
175  
176  	// Prompt for missing required vars after if check (avoid prompting if task won't run)
177  	prompted, err := e.promptTaskVars(t, call)
178  	if err != nil {
179  		return err
180  	}
181  	if prompted {
182  		// Recompile with the new vars
183  		t, err = e.FastCompiledTask(call)
184  		if err != nil {
185  			return err
186  		}
187  	}
188  
189  	if err := e.areTaskRequiredVarsSet(t); err != nil {
190  		return err
191  	}
192  
193  	if err := e.areTaskRequiredVarsAllowedValuesSet(t); err != nil {
194  		return err
195  	}
196  
197  	if !e.Watch && atomic.AddInt32(e.taskCallCount[t.Task], 1) >= MaximumTaskCall {

// go-task / go-task/executor.go:78,80
78  		concurrencySemaphore chan struct{}
79  		taskCallCount        map[string]*int32
80  		mkdirMutexMap        map[string]*sync.Mutex

59  // Terminate terminates all watchers and forwards.
60  func (f *Factory) Terminate() {
61  	f.mx.Lock()
62  	defer f.mx.Unlock()
63  
64  	if f.stopChan != nil {
65  		close(f.stopChan)
66  		f.stopChan = nil
67  	}
68  	for k := range f.factories {
69  		delete(f.factories, k)
70  	}
71  	f.forwarders.DeleteAll()
72  }

// lazygit / lazygit/pkg/gui/background.go:35,46,123
35  			go utils.Safe(self.startBackgroundFetch)
36  		} else {
37  			self.gui.c.Log.Errorf(
38  				"Value of config option 'refresher.fetchInterval' (%d) is invalid, disabling auto-fetch",
39  				fetchInterval)
40  		}
41  	}
42

44  		refreshInterval := userConfig.Refresher.RefreshInterval
45  		if refreshInterval > 0 {
46  			go utils.Safe(self.startBackgroundFilesRefresh)
47  		} else {
48  			self.gui.c.Log.Errorf(
49  				"Value of config option 'refresher.refreshInterval' (%d) is invalid, disabling auto-refresh",
50  				refreshInterval)
51  		}
52  	}
53  
54  	if self.gui.Config.GetDebug() {
55  		self.goEvery(time.Second*time.Duration(10), self.gui.stopChan, func(_ bool) error {
56  			formatBytes := func(b uint64) string {
57  				const unit = 1000
58  				if b < unit {
59  					return fmt.Sprintf("%d B", b)
60  				}
61  				div, exp := uint64(unit), 0
62  				for n := b / unit; n >= unit; n /= unit {
63  					div *= unit
64  					exp++
65  				}
66  				return fmt.Sprintf("%.1f %cB",
67  					float64(b)/float64(div), "kMGTPE"[exp])
68  			}
69  
70  			m := runtime.MemStats{}
71  			runtime.ReadMemStats(&m)
72  			self.gui.c.Log.Infof("Heap memory in use: %s", formatBytes(m.HeapAlloc))
73  			return nil
74  		})
75  	}
76  }
77  
78  func (self *BackgroundRoutineMgr) startBackgroundFetch() {
79  	self.gui.waitForIntro.Wait()
80  
81  	fetch := func(firstTimeOrRetriggered bool) error {
82  		// Do this on the UI thread so that we don't have to deal with synchronization around the
83  		// access of the repo state.
84  		self.gui.onUIThread(func() error {
85  			// There's a race here, where we might be recording the time stamp for a different repo
86  			// than where the fetch actually ran. It's not very likely though, and not harmful if it
87  			// does happen; guarding against it would be more effort than it's worth.
88  			self.gui.State.LastBackgroundFetchTime = time.Now()
89  			return nil
90  		})
91  
92  		if self.gui.UserConfig().Gui.ShowBottomLine || firstTimeOrRetriggered {
93  			return self.gui.helpers.AppStatus.WithWaitingStatusImpl(self.gui.Tr.FetchingStatus, func(gocui.Task) error {
94  				return self.backgroundFetch()
95  			}, nil)
96  		}
97  
98  		return self.backgroundFetch()
99  	}
100  
101  	// We want an immediate fetch at startup, and since goEvery starts by
102  	// waiting for the interval, we need to trigger one manually first
103  	_ = fetch(true)
104  
105  	userConfig := self.gui.UserConfig()
106  	self.triggerFetch = self.goEvery(userConfig.Refresher.FetchIntervalDuration(), self.gui.stopChan, fetch)
107  }
108  
109  func (self *BackgroundRoutineMgr) startBackgroundFilesRefresh() {
110  	self.gui.waitForIntro.Wait()
111  
112  	userConfig := self.gui.UserConfig()
113  	self.goEvery(userConfig.Refresher.RefreshIntervalDuration(), self.gui.stopChan, func(_ bool) error {
114  		self.gui.c.Refresh(types.RefreshOptions{Scope: []types.RefreshableView{types.FILES}})
115  		return nil
116  	})
117  }
118  
119  // returns a channel that can be used to trigger the callback immediately
120  func (self *BackgroundRoutineMgr) goEvery(interval time.Duration, stop chan struct{}, function func(bool) error) chan struct{} {
121  	done := make(chan struct{})
122  	retrigger := make(chan struct{})
123  	go utils.Safe(func() {

// lazygit / lazygit/pkg/gui/gui.go:89,955-982
89  	stopChan    chan struct{}
90  
91  	// when lazygit is opened outside a git directory we want to open to the most
92  	// recent repo with the recent repos popup showing
93  	showRecentRepos bool
94  
95  	Mutexes types.Mutexes
96  
97  	// when you enter into a submodule we'll append the superproject's path to this array
98  	// so that you can return to the superproject
99  	RepoPathStack *utils.StringStack
100  
101  	// this tells us whether our views have been initially set up
102  	ViewsSetup bool
103  
104  	Views types.Views
105  
106  	// Log of the commands/actions logged in the Command Log panel.
107  	GuiLog []string
108  
109  	// the extras window contains things like the command log
110  	ShowExtrasWindow bool
111  
112  	PopupHandler types.IPopupHandler
113  
114  	IsRefreshingFiles bool
115  
116  	// we use this to decide whether we'll return to the original directory that
117  	// lazygit was opened in, or if we'll retain the one we're currently in.
118  	RetainOriginalDir bool
119  
120  	// stores long-running operations associated with items (e.g. when a branch
121  	// is being pushed). At the moment the rule is to use an item operation when
122  	// we need to talk to the remote.
123  	itemOperations      map[string]types.ItemOperation
124  	itemOperationsMutex deadlock.Mutex
125  
126  	PrevLayout PrevLayout
127  
128  	// this is the initial dir we are in upon opening lazygit. We hold onto this
129  	// in case we want to restore it before quitting for users who have set up
130  	// the feature for changing directory upon quit.
131  	// The reason we don't just wait until quit time to handle changing directories
132  	// is because some users want to keep track of the current lazygit directory in an outside
133  	// process
134  	InitialDir string
135  
136  	BackgroundRoutineMgr *BackgroundRoutineMgr
137  	// for accessing the gui's state from outside this package
138  	stateAccessor *StateAccessor
139  
140  	Updating bool
141  
142  	c       *helpers.HelperCommon
143  	helpers *helpers.Helpers
144  
145  	previousLanguageConfig string
146  
147  	integrationTest integrationTypes.IntegrationTest
148  
149  	afterLayoutFuncs chan func() error
150  }
151

154  }

160  }

163  	return self.gui.Updating
164  }

167  	self.gui.Updating = value
168  }

171  	return self.gui.State
172  }

175  	return self.gui.pagerConfig
176  }

179  	return self.gui.IsRefreshingFiles
180  }

184  }

187  	return self.gui.ShowExtrasWindow
188  }
189

191  	self.gui.ShowExtrasWindow = value
192  }
193  
194  func (self *StateAccessor) GetRetainOriginalDir() bool {
195  	return self.gui.RetainOriginalDir
196  }
197  
198  func (self *StateAccessor) SetRetainOriginalDir(value bool) {
199  	self.gui.RetainOriginalDir = value
200  }
201  
202  func (self *StateAccessor) GetItemOperation(item types.HasUrn) types.ItemOperation {
203  	self.gui.itemOperationsMutex.Lock()
204  	defer self.gui.itemOperationsMutex.Unlock()
205  
206  	return self.gui.itemOperations[item.URN()]
207  }
208  
209  func (self *StateAccessor) SetItemOperation(item types.HasUrn, operation types.ItemOperation) {
210  	self.gui.itemOperationsMutex.Lock()
211  	defer self.gui.itemOperationsMutex.Unlock()
212  
213  	self.gui.itemOperations[item.URN()] = operation
214  }
215  
216  func (self *StateAccessor) ClearItemOperation(item types.HasUrn) {
217  	self.gui.itemOperationsMutex.Lock()
218  	defer self.gui.itemOperationsMutex.Unlock()
219  
220  	delete(self.gui.itemOperations, item.URN())
221  }

223  // we keep track of some stuff from one render to the next to see if certain
224  // things have changed
225  type PrevLayout struct {
226  	Information string
227  	MainWidth   int
228  	MainHeight  int
229  }

233  	Modes *types.Modes

236

239  
240  	ContextMgr *ContextMgr
241  	Contexts   *context.ContextTree
242  
243  	// WindowViewNameMap is a mapping of windows to the current view of that window.
244  	// Some views move between windows for example the commitFiles view and when cycling through
245  	// side windows we need to know which view to give focus to for a given window
246  	WindowViewNameMap *utils.ThreadSafeMap[string, string]
247  
248  	// tells us whether we've set up our views for the current repo. We'll need to
249  	// do this whenever we switch back and forth between repos to get the views
250  	// back in sync with the repo state
251  	ViewsSetup bool
252  
253  	ScreenMode types.ScreenMode
254  
255  	CurrentPopupOpts *types.CreatePopupPanelOpts
256  
257  	LastBackgroundFetchTime time.Time
258  }

309  
310  func (gui *Gui) onSwitchToNewRepo(startArgs appTypes.StartArgs, contextKey types.ContextKey) error {
311  	err := gui.onNewRepo(startArgs, contextKey)
312  	if err == nil && gui.UserConfig().Git.AutoFetch && gui.UserConfig().Refresher.FetchInterval > 0 {
313  		if time.Since(gui.State.LastBackgroundFetchTime) > gui.UserConfig().Refresher.FetchIntervalDuration() {
314  			gui.BackgroundRoutineMgr.triggerImmediateFetch()
315  		}
316  	}
317  	return err
318  }
319  
320  func (gui *Gui) onNewRepo(startArgs appTypes.StartArgs, contextKey types.ContextKey) error {
321  	var err error
322  	gui.git, err = commands.NewGitCommand(
323  		gui.Common,
324  		gui.gitVersion,
325  		gui.os,
326  		git_config.NewStdCachedGitConfig(gui.Log),
327  		gui.pagerConfig,
328  	)
329  	if err != nil {
330  		return err
331  	}
332  
333  	err = gui.Config.ReloadUserConfigForRepo(gui.getPerRepoConfigFiles())
334  	if err != nil {
335  		return err
336  	}
337  
338  	err = gui.onUserConfigLoaded()
339  	if err != nil {
340  		return err
341  	}
342  
343  	contextToPush := gui.resetState(startArgs)
344  
345  	gui.resetHelpersAndControllers()
346  
347  	if err := gui.resetKeybindings(); err != nil {
348  		return err
349  	}
350  
351  	gui.g.SetFocusHandler(func(Focused bool) error {
352  		if Focused {
353  			gui.git.Config.DropConfigCache()
354  
355  			oldConfig := gui.Config.GetUserConfig()
356  			reloadErr, didChange := gui.Config.ReloadChangedUserConfigFiles()
357  			if didChange && reloadErr == nil {
358  				gui.c.Log.Info("User config changed - reloading")
359  				reloadErr = gui.onUserConfigLoaded()
360  				if err := gui.resetKeybindings(); err != nil {
361  					return err
362  				}
363  
364  				if err := gui.checkForChangedConfigsThatDontAutoReload(oldConfig, gui.Config.GetUserConfig()); err != nil {
365  					return err
366  				}
367  			}
368  
369  			gui.c.Log.Info("Receiving focus - refreshing")
370  			gui.helpers.Refresh.Refresh(types.RefreshOptions{Mode: types.ASYNC})
371  			return reloadErr
372  		}
373  
374  		return nil
375  	})
376  
377  	gui.g.SetOpenHyperlinkFunc(func(url string, viewname string) error {
378  		if strings.HasPrefix(url, "lazygit-edit:") {
379  			re := regexp.MustCompile(`^lazygit-edit://(.+?)(?::(\d*))?$`)
380  			matches := re.FindStringSubmatch(url)
381  			if matches == nil {
382  				return fmt.Errorf(gui.Tr.InvalidLazygitEditURL, url)
383  			}
384  			filepath := matches[1]
385  			if matches[2] != "" {
386  				lineNumber := utils.MustConvertToInt(matches[2])
387  				lineNumber = gui.helpers.Diff.AdjustLineNumber(filepath, lineNumber, viewname)
388  				return gui.helpers.Files.EditFileAtLine(filepath, lineNumber)
389  			}
390  			return gui.helpers.Files.EditFiles([]string{filepath})
391  		}
392  
393  		if err := gui.os.OpenLink(url); err != nil {
394  			return fmt.Errorf(gui.Tr.FailedToOpenURL, url, err)
395  		}
396  
397  		return nil
398  	})
399  
400  	gui.g.SetOnSelectSearchResultFunc(func(v *gocui.View, selectedLineIdx int) {
401  		ctx, ok := gui.helpers.View.ContextForView(v.Name())
402  		if ok {
403  			if searchableContext, ok := ctx.(types.ISearchableContext); ok {
404  				searchableContext.OnSearchSelect(selectedLineIdx)
405  			}
406  		}
407  	})
408  
409  	gui.g.SetRenderSearchStatusFunc(func(v *gocui.View, index int, total int) {
410  		ctx, ok := gui.helpers.View.ContextForView(v.Name())
411  		if ok {
412  			if searchableContext, ok := ctx.(types.ISearchableContext); ok {
413  				searchableContext.RenderSearchStatus(index, total)
414  			}
415  		}
416  	})
417  
418  	// if a context key has been given, push that instead, and set its index to 0
419  	if contextKey != context.NO_CONTEXT {
420  		contextToPush = gui.c.ContextForKey(contextKey)
421  		// when we pass a list context, the expectation is that our cursor goes to the top,
422  		// because e.g. with worktrees, we'll show the current worktree at the top of the list.
423  		listContext, ok := contextToPush.(types.IListContext)
424  		if ok {
425  			listContext.GetList().SetSelection(0)
426  		}
427  	}
428  
429  	gui.c.Context().Push(contextToPush, types.OnFocusOpts{})
430  
431  	gui.render()
432  
433  	return nil
434  }
435  
436  func (gui *Gui) getPerRepoConfigFiles() []*config.ConfigFile {
437  	repoConfigFiles := []*config.ConfigFile{
438  		// TODO: add filepath.Join(gui.git.RepoPaths.RepoPath(), ".lazygit.yml"),
439  		// with trust prompt
440  		{
441  			Path:   filepath.Join(gui.git.RepoPaths.RepoGitDirPath(), "lazygit.yml"),
442  			Policy: config.ConfigFilePolicySkipIfMissing,
443  		},
444  	}
445  
446  	prevDir := gui.c.Git().RepoPaths.RepoPath()
447  	dir := filepath.Dir(prevDir)
448  	for dir != prevDir {
449  		repoConfigFiles = utils.Prepend(repoConfigFiles, &config.ConfigFile{
450  			Path:   filepath.Join(dir, ".lazygit.yml"),
451  			Policy: config.ConfigFilePolicySkipIfMissing,
452  		})
453  		prevDir = dir
454  		dir = filepath.Dir(dir)
455  	}
456  	return repoConfigFiles
457  }
458  
459  func (gui *Gui) onUserConfigLoaded() error {
460  	userConfig := gui.Config.GetUserConfig()
461  	gui.Common.SetUserConfig(userConfig)
462  
463  	if gui.previousLanguageConfig != userConfig.Gui.Language {
464  		tr, err := i18n.NewTranslationSetFromConfig(gui.Log, userConfig.Gui.Language)
465  		if err != nil {
466  			return err
467  		}
468  		gui.c.Tr = tr
469  		gui.previousLanguageConfig = userConfig.Gui.Language
470  	}
471  
472  	gui.setColorScheme()
473  	gui.configureViewProperties()
474  
475  	gui.g.SearchEscapeKey = config.GetValidatedKeyBindingKey(userConfig.Keybinding.Universal.Return)
476  	gui.g.NextSearchMatchKey = config.GetValidatedKeyBindingKey(userConfig.Keybinding.Universal.NextMatch)
477  	gui.g.PrevSearchMatchKey = config.GetValidatedKeyBindingKey(userConfig.Keybinding.Universal.PrevMatch)
478  
479  	gui.g.SetEditKeybindings(
480  		config.GetValidatedKeyBindingKey(userConfig.Keybinding.Universal.MoveWordLeft),
481  		config.GetValidatedKeyBindingKey(userConfig.Keybinding.Universal.MoveWordRight),
482  		config.GetValidatedKeyBindingKey(userConfig.Keybinding.Universal.BackspaceWord),
483  		config.GetValidatedKeyBindingKey(userConfig.Keybinding.Universal.ForwardDeleteWord),
484  	)
485  
486  	gui.g.ShowListFooter = userConfig.Gui.ShowListFooter
487  
488  	gui.g.Mouse = userConfig.Gui.MouseEvents
489  
490  	// originally we could only hide the command log permanently via the config
491  	// but now we do it via state. So we need to still support the config for the
492  	// sake of backwards compatibility. We're making use of short circuiting here
493  	gui.ShowExtrasWindow = userConfig.Gui.ShowCommandLog && !gui.c.GetAppState().HideCommandLog
494  
495  	authors.SetCustomAuthors(userConfig.Gui.AuthorColors)
496  	if userConfig.Gui.NerdFontsVersion != "" {
497  		icons.SetNerdFontsVersion(userConfig.Gui.NerdFontsVersion)
498  	} else if userConfig.Gui.ShowIcons {
499  		icons.SetNerdFontsVersion("2")
500  	} else {
501  		icons.SetNerdFontsVersion("")
502  	}
503  
504  	if len(userConfig.Gui.BranchColorPatterns) > 0 {
505  		presentation.SetCustomBranches(userConfig.Gui.BranchColorPatterns, true)
506  	} else {
507  		// Fall back to the deprecated branchColors config
508  		presentation.SetCustomBranches(userConfig.Gui.BranchColors, false)
509  	}
510  
511  	return nil
512  }
513  
514  func (gui *Gui) checkForChangedConfigsThatDontAutoReload(oldConfig *config.UserConfig, newConfig *config.UserConfig) error {
515  	configsThatDontAutoReload := []string{
516  		"Git.AutoFetch",
517  		"Git.AutoRefresh",
518  		"Refresher.RefreshInterval",
519  		"Refresher.FetchInterval",
520  		"Update.Method",
521  		"Update.Days",
522  	}
523  
524  	changedConfigs := []string{}
525  	for _, config := range configsThatDontAutoReload {
526  		old := reflect.ValueOf(oldConfig).Elem()
527  		new := reflect.ValueOf(newConfig).Elem()
528  		fieldNames := strings.Split(config, ".")
529  		userFacingPath := make([]string, 0, len(fieldNames))
530  		// navigate to the leaves in old and new config
531  		for _, fieldName := range fieldNames {
532  			f, _ := old.Type().FieldByName(fieldName)
533  			userFacingName := f.Tag.Get("yaml")
534  			if userFacingName == "" {
535  				userFacingName = fieldName
536  			}
537  			userFacingPath = append(userFacingPath, userFacingName)
538  			old = old.FieldByName(fieldName)
539  			new = new.FieldByName(fieldName)
540  		}
541  		// if the value has changed, ...
542  		if !old.Equal(new) {
543  			// ... append it to the list of changed configs
544  			changedConfigs = append(changedConfigs, strings.Join(userFacingPath, "."))
545  		}
546  	}
547  
548  	if len(changedConfigs) == 0 {
549  		return nil
550  	}
551  
552  	message := utils.ResolvePlaceholderString(
553  		gui.c.Tr.NonReloadableConfigWarning,
554  		map[string]string{
555  			"configs": strings.Join(changedConfigs, "\n"),
556  		},
557  	)
558  	gui.c.Confirm(types.ConfirmOpts{
559  		Title:  gui.c.Tr.NonReloadableConfigWarningTitle,
560  		Prompt: message,
561  	})
562  
563  	return nil
564  }
565  
566  // resetState reuses the repo state from our repo state map, if the repo was
567  // open before; otherwise it creates a new one.
568  func (gui *Gui) resetState(startArgs appTypes.StartArgs) types.Context {
569  	// Un-highlight the current view if there is one. The reason we do this is
570  	// that the repo we are switching to might have a different view focused,
571  	// and would then show an inactive highlight for the previous view.
572  	if oldCurrentView := gui.g.CurrentView(); oldCurrentView != nil {
573  		oldCurrentView.Highlight = false
574  	}
575  
576  	worktreePath := gui.git.RepoPaths.WorktreePath()
577  
578  	if state := gui.RepoStateMap[Repo(worktreePath)]; state != nil {
579  		gui.State = state
580  		gui.State.ViewsSetup = false
581  
582  		contextTree := gui.State.Contexts
583  		gui.State.WindowViewNameMap = initialWindowViewNameMap(contextTree)
584  
585  		// setting this to nil so we don't get stuck based on a popup that was
586  		// previously opened
587  		gui.Mutexes.PopupMutex.Lock()
588  		gui.State.CurrentPopupOpts = nil
589  		gui.Mutexes.PopupMutex.Unlock()
590  
591  		return gui.c.Context().Current()
592  	}
593  
594  	contextTree := gui.contextTree()
595  
596  	initialScreenMode := initialScreenMode(startArgs, gui.Config)
597  
598  	gui.State = &GuiRepoState{
599  		ViewsSetup: false,
600  		Model: &types.Model{
601  			CommitFiles:           nil,
602  			Files:                 make([]*models.File, 0),
603  			Commits:               make([]*models.Commit, 0),
604  			StashEntries:          make([]*models.StashEntry, 0),
605  			FilteredReflogCommits: make([]*models.Commit, 0),
606  			ReflogCommits:         make([]*models.Commit, 0),
607  			BisectInfo:            git_commands.NewNullBisectInfo(),
608  			FilesTrie:             patricia.NewTrie(),
609  			Authors:               map[string]*models.Author{},
610  			MainBranches:          git_commands.NewMainBranches(gui.c.Common, gui.os.Cmd),
611  			HashPool:              &utils.StringPool{},
612  			PullRequests:          gui.loadCachedPullRequests(),
613  			PullRequestsMap:       make(map[string]*models.GithubPullRequest),
614  		},
615  		Modes: &types.Modes{
616  			Filtering:        filtering.New(startArgs.FilterPath, ""),
617  			CherryPicking:    cherrypicking.New(),
618  			Diffing:          diffing.New(),
619  			MarkedBaseCommit: marked_base_commit.New(),
620  		},
621  		ScreenMode: initialScreenMode,
622  		// TODO: only use contexts from context manager
623  		ContextMgr:        NewContextMgr(gui, contextTree),
624  		Contexts:          contextTree,
625  		WindowViewNameMap: initialWindowViewNameMap(contextTree),
626  		SearchState:       types.NewSearchState(),
627  	}
628  
629  	gui.RepoStateMap[Repo(worktreePath)] = gui.State
630  
631  	return initialContext(contextTree, startArgs)
632  }
633  
634  func (gui *Gui) loadCachedPullRequests() []*models.GithubPullRequest {
635  	repoPath := gui.git.RepoPaths.RepoPath()
636  	cachedPRs := gui.c.GetAppState().GithubPullRequests[repoPath]
637  
638  	return lo.Map(cachedPRs, func(cached config.CachedPullRequest, _ int) *models.GithubPullRequest {
639  		return &models.GithubPullRequest{
640  			HeadRefName: cached.HeadRefName,
641  			Number:      cached.Number,
642  			Title:       cached.Title,
643  			State:       cached.State,
644  			Url:         cached.Url,
645  			HeadRepositoryOwner: models.GithubRepositoryOwner{
646  				Login: cached.HeadRepositoryOwner,
647  			},
648  		}
649  	})
650  }
651  
652  func (gui *Gui) getViewBufferManagerForView(view *gocui.View) *tasks.ViewBufferManager {
653  	manager, ok := gui.viewBufferManagerMap[view.Name()]
654  	if !ok {
655  		return nil
656  	}
657  
658  	return manager
659  }
660  
661  func initialWindowViewNameMap(contextTree *context.ContextTree) *utils.ThreadSafeMap[string, string] {
662  	result := utils.NewThreadSafeMap[string, string]()
663  
664  	for _, context := range contextTree.Flatten() {
665  		result.Set(context.GetWindowName(), context.GetViewName())
666  	}
667  
668  	return result
669  }
670  
671  func initialScreenMode(startArgs appTypes.StartArgs, config config.AppConfigurer) types.ScreenMode {
672  	if startArgs.ScreenMode != "" {
673  		return parseScreenModeArg(startArgs.ScreenMode)
674  	} else if startArgs.FilterPath != "" || startArgs.GitArg != appTypes.GitArgNone {
675  		return types.SCREEN_HALF
676  	}
677  
678  	return parseScreenModeArg(config.GetUserConfig().Gui.ScreenMode)
679  }
680  
681  func parseScreenModeArg(screenModeArg string) types.ScreenMode {
682  	switch screenModeArg {
683  	case "half":
684  		return types.SCREEN_HALF
685  	case "full":
686  		return types.SCREEN_FULL
687  	default:
688  		return types.SCREEN_NORMAL
689  	}
690  }
691  
692  func initialContext(contextTree *context.ContextTree, startArgs appTypes.StartArgs) types.IListContext {
693  	var initialContext types.IListContext = contextTree.Files
694  
695  	if startArgs.FilterPath != "" {
696  		initialContext = contextTree.LocalCommits
697  	} else if startArgs.GitArg != appTypes.GitArgNone {
698  		switch startArgs.GitArg {
699  		case appTypes.GitArgStatus:
700  			initialContext = contextTree.Files
701  		case appTypes.GitArgBranch:
702  			initialContext = contextTree.Branches
703  		case appTypes.GitArgLog:
704  			initialContext = contextTree.LocalCommits
705  		case appTypes.GitArgStash:
706  			initialContext = contextTree.Stash
707  		default:
708  			panic("unhandled git arg")
709  		}
710  	}
711  
712  	return initialContext
713  }
714  
715  func (gui *Gui) Contexts() *context.ContextTree {
716  	return gui.State.Contexts
717  }
718  
719  // for now the split view will always be on
720  // NewGui builds a new gui handler
721  func NewGui(
722  	cmn *common.Common,
723  	configurer config.AppConfigurer,
724  	gitVersion *git_commands.GitVersion,
725  	updater *updates.Updater,
726  	showRecentRepos bool,
727  	initialDir string,
728  	test integrationTypes.IntegrationTest,
729  ) (*Gui, error) {
730  	gui := &Gui{
731  		Common:               cmn,
732  		gitVersion:           gitVersion,
733  		Config:               configurer,
734  		Updater:              updater,
735  		statusManager:        status.NewStatusManager(),
736  		viewBufferManagerMap: map[string]*tasks.ViewBufferManager{},
737  		viewPtmxMap:          map[string]*os.File{},
738  		showRecentRepos:      showRecentRepos,
739  		RepoPathStack:        &utils.StringStack{},
740  		RepoStateMap:         map[Repo]*GuiRepoState{},
741  		GuiLog:               []string{},
742  
743  		// initializing this to true for the time being; it will be reset to the
744  		// real value after loading the user config:
745  		ShowExtrasWindow: true,
746  
747  		InitialDir:       initialDir,
748  		afterLayoutFuncs: make(chan func() error, 1000),
749  
750  		itemOperations: make(map[string]types.ItemOperation),
751  	}
752  
753  	gui.PopupHandler = popup.NewPopupHandler(
754  		cmn,
755  		func(ctx goContext.Context, opts types.CreatePopupPanelOpts) {
756  			gui.helpers.Confirmation.CreatePopupPanel(ctx, opts)
757  		},
758  		func() error { gui.c.Refresh(types.RefreshOptions{Mode: types.ASYNC}); return nil },
759  		func() { gui.State.ContextMgr.Pop() },
760  		func() types.Context { return gui.State.ContextMgr.Current() },
761  		gui.createMenu,
762  		func(message string, f func(gocui.Task) error) { gui.helpers.AppStatus.WithWaitingStatus(message, f) },
763  		func(message string, f func() error) error {
764  			return gui.helpers.AppStatus.WithWaitingStatusSync(message, f)
765  		},
766  		func(message string, kind types.ToastKind) { gui.helpers.AppStatus.Toast(message, kind) },
767  		func() string { return gui.Views.Prompt.TextArea.GetContent() },
768  		func() bool { return gui.c.InDemo() },
769  	)
770  
771  	guiCommon := &guiCommon{gui: gui, IPopupHandler: gui.PopupHandler}
772  	helperCommon := &helpers.HelperCommon{IGuiCommon: guiCommon, Common: cmn, IGetContexts: gui}
773  
774  	credentialsHelper := helpers.NewCredentialsHelper(helperCommon)
775  
776  	guiIO := oscommands.NewGuiIO(
777  		cmn.Log,
778  		gui.LogCommand,
779  		gui.getCmdWriter,
780  		credentialsHelper.PromptUserForCredential,
781  	)
782  
783  	osCommand := oscommands.NewOSCommand(cmn, configurer, oscommands.GetPlatform(), guiIO)
784  
785  	gui.os = osCommand
786  
787  	// storing this stuff on the gui for now to ease refactoring
788  	// TODO: reset these controllers upon changing repos due to state changing
789  	gui.c = helperCommon
790  
791  	gui.BackgroundRoutineMgr = &BackgroundRoutineMgr{gui: gui}
792  	gui.stateAccessor = &StateAccessor{gui: gui}
793  
794  	gui.pagerConfig = config.NewPagerConfig(func() *config.UserConfig { return gui.UserConfig() })
795  
796  	return gui, nil
797  }

805  func (gui *Gui) initGocui(headless bool, test integrationTypes.IntegrationTest) (*gocui.Gui, error) {
806  	runInSandbox := os.Getenv(components.SANDBOX_ENV_VAR) == "true"
807  	playRecording := test != nil && !runInSandbox
808  
809  	width, height := 0, 0
810  	if test != nil {
811  		if test.RequiresHeadless() {
812  			if runInSandbox {
813  				panic("Test requires headless, can't run in sandbox")
814  			}
815  			headless = true
816  		}
817  		width, height = test.HeadlessDimensions()
818  	}
819  
820  	g, err := gocui.NewGui(gocui.NewGuiOpts{
821  		OutputMode:       gocui.OutputTrue,
822  		SupportOverlaps:  OverlappingEdges,
823  		PlayRecording:    playRecording,
824  		Headless:         headless,
825  		RuneReplacements: RuneReplacements,
826  		Width:            width,
827  		Height:           height,
828  	})
829  	if err != nil {
830  		return nil, err
831  	}
832  
833  	return g, nil
834  }

881  // Run: setup the gui with keybindings and start the mainloop
882  func (gui *Gui) Run(startArgs appTypes.StartArgs) error {
883  	g, err := gui.initGocui(Headless(), startArgs.IntegrationTest)
884  	if err != nil {
885  		return err
886  	}
887  
888  	gui.g = g
889  	defer gui.g.Close()
890  
891  	g.ErrorHandler = gui.PopupHandler.ErrorHandler
892  
893  	gui.g.ShouldHandleMouseEvent = func(view *gocui.View, key gocui.KeyName) bool {
894  		if gui.helpers.Confirmation.IsPopupPanelFocused() && gui.currentViewName() != view.Name() &&
895  			!gocui.IsMouseScrollKey(key) {
896  			// we ignore click events on views that aren't popup panels, when a popup panel is focused.
897  			// Unless both the current view and the clicked-on view are either commit message or commit
898  			// description, or a prompt and the suggestions view, because we want to allow switching
899  			// between those two views by clicking.
900  			isCommitMessageOrSuggestionsView := func(viewName string) bool {
901  				return viewName == "commitMessage" || viewName == "commitDescription" ||
902  					viewName == "prompt" || viewName == "suggestions"
903  			}
904  			if !isCommitMessageOrSuggestionsView(gui.currentViewName()) || !isCommitMessageOrSuggestionsView(view.Name()) {
905  				return false
906  			}
907  		}
908  
909  		return true
910  	}
911  
912  	// if the deadlock package wants to report a deadlock, we first need to
913  	// close the gui so that we can actually read what it prints.
914  	deadlock.Opts.LogBuf = utils.NewOnceWriter(os.Stderr, func() {
915  		gui.g.Close()
916  	})
917  	// disable deadlock reporting if we're not running in debug mode, or if
918  	// we're debugging an integration test. In this latter case, stopping at
919  	// breakpoints and stepping through code can easily take more than 30s.
920  	deadlock.Opts.Disable = !gui.Debug || os.Getenv(components.WAIT_FOR_DEBUGGER_ENV_VAR) != ""
921  
922  	gui.g.OnSearchEscape = func() error { gui.helpers.Search.Cancel(); return nil }
923  
924  	gui.g.SetManager(gocui.ManagerFunc(gui.layout))
925  
926  	if err := gui.createAllViews(); err != nil {
927  		return err
928  	}
929  
930  	// onNewRepo must be called after g.SetManager because SetManager deletes keybindings
931  	if err := gui.onNewRepo(startArgs, context.NO_CONTEXT); err != nil {
932  		return err
933  	}
934  
935  	gui.waitForIntro.Add(1)
936  
937  	gui.BackgroundRoutineMgr.startBackgroundRoutines()
938  
939  	gui.Helpers().SuspendResume.InstallResumeSignalHandler()
940  
941  	gui.c.Log.Info("starting main loop")
942  
943  	// setting here so we can use it in layout.go
944  	gui.integrationTest = startArgs.IntegrationTest
945  
946  	err = gui.g.MainLoop()
947  	if errors.Is(err, gocui.ErrQuit) {
948  		// Give the focused context a chance to clean up before we tear down the app.
949  		gui.c.Context().Current().HandleQuit()
950  	}
951  	return err
952  }
953  
954  func (gui *Gui) RunAndHandleError(startArgs appTypes.StartArgs) error {
955  	gui.stopChan = make(chan struct{})
956  	return utils.SafeWithError(func() error {
957  		if err := gui.Run(startArgs); err != nil {
958  			for _, manager := range gui.viewBufferManagerMap {
959  				manager.Close()
960  			}
961  
962  			close(gui.stopChan)
963  
964  			if errors.Is(err, gocui.ErrQuit) {
965  				if gui.c.State().GetRetainOriginalDir() {
966  					if err := gui.helpers.RecordDirectory.RecordDirectory(gui.InitialDir); err != nil {
967  						return err
968  					}
969  				} else {
970  					if err := gui.helpers.RecordDirectory.RecordCurrentDirectory(); err != nil {
971  						return err
972  					}
973  				}
974  
975  				return nil
976  			}
977  
978  			return err
979  		}
980  
981  		return nil
982  	})

// lazygit / lazygit/pkg/tasks/tasks.go:338-357
338  // Close closes the task manager, killing whatever task may currently be running
339  func (self *ViewBufferManager) Close() {
340  	if self.stopCurrentTask == nil {
341  		return
342  	}
343  
344  	c := make(chan struct{})
345  
346  	go utils.Safe(func() {
347  		self.stopCurrentTask()
348  		c <- struct{}{}
349  	})
350  
351  	select {
352  	case <-c:
353  		return
354  	case <-time.After(3 * time.Second):
355  		fmt.Println("cannot kill child process")
356  	}
357  }

// k9s / opencode/cmd/root.go:130,252
130  func loadConfiguration() (*config.Config, error) {
131  	slog.Info("🐶 K9s starting up...")
132  
133  	k8sCfg := client.NewConfig(k8sFlags)
134  	k9sCfg := config.NewConfig(k8sCfg)
135  	var errs error
136  
137  	conn, err := client.InitConnection(k8sCfg, slog.Default())
138  	if err != nil {
139  		errs = errors.Join(errs, err)
140  	}
141  	k9sCfg.SetConnection(conn)
142  
143  	if err := k9sCfg.Load(config.AppConfigFile, false); err != nil {
144  		errs = errors.Join(errs, err)
145  	}
146  	k9sCfg.K9s.Override(k9sFlags)
147  	if err := k9sCfg.Refine(k8sFlags, k9sFlags, k8sCfg); err != nil {
148  		slog.Error("Fail to refine k9s config", slogs.Error, err)
149  		errs = errors.Join(errs, err)
150  	}
151  
152  	// Try to access server version if that fail. Connectivity issue?
153  	if !conn.CheckConnectivity() {
154  		errs = errors.Join(errs, fmt.Errorf("cannot connect to context: %s", k9sCfg.K9s.ActiveContextName()))
155  	}
156  	if !conn.ConnectionOK() {
157  		slog.Warn("💣 Kubernetes connectivity toast!")
158  		errs = errors.Join(errs, fmt.Errorf("k8s connection failed for context: %s", k9sCfg.K9s.ActiveContextName()))
159  	} else {
160  		slog.Info("✅ Kubernetes connectivity OK")
161  	}
162  
163  	if err := k9sCfg.Save(false); err != nil {
164  		slog.Error("K9s config save failed", slogs.Error, err)
165  		errs = errors.Join(errs, err)
166  	}
167  
168  	return k9sCfg, errs
169  }
170  
171  func parseLevel(level string) slog.Level {
172  	switch level {
173  	case "debug":
174  		return slog.LevelDebug
175  	case "warn":
176  		return slog.LevelWarn
177  	case "error":
178  		return slog.LevelError
179  	default:
180  		return slog.LevelInfo
181  	}
182  }

77  		}

80  		close(sub)

87  // srcOrDstKey turns a directory entry into a sort key using the defined transforms.
88  func (m *March) srcOrDstKey(entry fs.DirEntry, isSrc bool) string {
89  	if entry == nil {
90  		return ""
91  	}
92  	name := path.Base(entry.Remote())
93  	_, isDirectory := entry.(fs.Directory)
94  	if isSrc {
95  		name = transform.Path(m.Ctx, name, isDirectory)
96  	}
97  	for _, transform := range m.transforms {
98  		name = transform(name)
99  	}
100  	// Suffix entries to make identically named files and
101  	// directories sort consistently with directories first.
102  	if isDirectory {
103  		name += "D"
104  	} else {
105  		name += "F"
106  	}
107  	return name
108  }
109  
110  // srcKey turns a directory entry into a sort key using the defined transforms.
111  func (m *March) srcKey(entry fs.DirEntry) string {
112  	return m.srcOrDstKey(entry, true)
113  }
114  
115  // dstKey turns a directory entry into a sort key using the defined transforms.
116  func (m *March) dstKey(entry fs.DirEntry) string {
117  	return m.srcOrDstKey(entry, false)
118  }
119  
120  // makeListDir makes constructs a listing function for the given fs
121  // and includeAll flags for marching through the file system.
122  // Note: this will optionally flag filter-aware backends!
123  func (m *March) makeListDir(ctx context.Context, f fs.Fs, includeAll bool, keyFn list.KeyFn) listDirFn {
124  	ci := fs.GetConfig(ctx)
125  	fi := filter.GetConfig(ctx)
126  	if !(ci.UseListR && f.Features().ListR != nil) && // !--fast-list active and
127  		!(ci.NoTraverse && fi.HaveFilesFrom()) { // !(--files-from and --no-traverse)
128  		return func(dir string, callback fs.ListRCallback) (err error) {
129  			dirCtx := filter.SetUseFilter(m.Ctx, f.Features().FilterAware && !includeAll) // make filter-aware backends constrain List
130  			return list.DirSortedFn(dirCtx, f, includeAll, dir, callback, keyFn)
131  		}
132  	}
133  
134  	// This returns a closure for use when --fast-list is active or for when
135  	// --files-from and --no-traverse is set
136  	var (
137  		mu      sync.Mutex
138  		started bool
139  		dirs    dirtree.DirTree
140  		dirsErr error
141  	)
142  	return func(dir string, callback fs.ListRCallback) (err error) {
143  		mu.Lock()
144  		if !started {
145  			dirCtx := filter.SetUseFilter(m.Ctx, f.Features().FilterAware && !includeAll) // make filter-aware backends constrain List
146  			dirs, dirsErr = walk.NewDirTree(dirCtx, f, m.Dir, includeAll, ci.MaxDepth)
147  			started = true
148  		}
149  		if dirsErr != nil {
150  			mu.Unlock()
151  			return dirsErr
152  		}
153  		entries, ok := dirs[dir]
154  		if !ok {
155  			mu.Unlock()
156  			return fs.ErrorDirNotFound
157  		}
158  		delete(dirs, dir)
159  		mu.Unlock()
160  
161  		// We use a stable sort here just in case there are
162  		// duplicates. Assuming the remote delivers the entries in a
163  		// consistent order, this will give the best user experience
164  		// in syncing as it will use the first entry for the sync
165  		// comparison.
166  		slices.SortStableFunc(entries, func(a, b fs.DirEntry) int {
167  			return cmp.Compare(keyFn(a), keyFn(b))
168  		})
169  		return callback(entries)
170  	}
171  }
172  
173  // listDirJob describe a directory listing that needs to be done
174  type listDirJob struct {
175  	srcRemote string
176  	dstRemote string
177  	srcDepth  int
178  	dstDepth  int
179  	noSrc     bool
180  	noDst     bool
181  }
182  
183  // Run starts the matching process off
184  func (m *March) Run(ctx context.Context) error {
185  	ci := fs.GetConfig(ctx)
186  	fi := filter.GetConfig(ctx)
187  	m.init(ctx)
188  
189  	srcDepth := ci.MaxDepth
190  	if srcDepth < 0 {
191  		srcDepth = fs.MaxLevel
192  	}
193  	dstDepth := srcDepth
194  	if fi.Opt.DeleteExcluded {
195  		dstDepth = fs.MaxLevel
196  	}
197  
198  	var mu sync.Mutex // Protects vars below
199  	var jobError error
200  	var errCount int
201  
202  	// Start some directory listing go routines
203  	var wg sync.WaitGroup         // sync closing of go routines
204  	var traversing sync.WaitGroup // running directory traversals
205  	checkers := ci.Checkers
206  	in := make(chan listDirJob, checkers)
207  	for range checkers {
208  		wg.Go(func() {
209  			for {
210  				select {
211  				case <-m.Ctx.Done():
212  					return
213  				case job, ok := <-in:
214  					if !ok {
215  						return
216  					}
217  					jobs, err := m.processJob(job)
218  					if err != nil {
219  						mu.Lock()
220  						// Keep reference only to the first encountered error
221  						if jobError == nil {
222  							jobError = err
223  						}
224  						errCount++
225  						mu.Unlock()
226  					}
227  					if len(jobs) > 0 {
228  						traversing.Add(len(jobs))
229  						go func() {
230  							// Now we have traversed this directory, send these
231  							// jobs off for traversal in the background
232  							for _, newJob := range jobs {
233  								select {
234  								case <-m.Ctx.Done():
235  									// discard job if finishing
236  									traversing.Done()
237  								case in <- newJob:
238  								}
239  							}
240  						}()
241  					}
242  					traversing.Done()
243  				}
244  			}
245  		})
246  	}

// rclone / rclone/lib/batcher/batcher.go:50,112

54  // request holds an incoming request with a place for a reply
55  type request[Item, Result any] struct {
56  	item   Item
57  	name   string
58  	result chan<- response[Result]
59  	quit   bool // if set then quit
60  }
61

63  // for a batch to complete.
64  type response[Result any] struct {
65  	err   error
66  	entry Result
67  }
68  
69  // New creates a Batcher for Item and Result calling commit to do the actual committing.
70  func New[Item, Result any](ctx context.Context, f any, commit CommitBatchFn[Item, Result], opt Options) (*Batcher[Item, Result], error) {
71  	// fs.Debugf(f, "Creating batcher with mode %q, size %d, timeout %v", mode, size, timeout)
72  	if opt.Size > opt.MaxBatchSize || opt.Size < 0 {
73  		return nil, fmt.Errorf("batcher: batch size must be < %d and >= 0 - it is currently %d", opt.MaxBatchSize, opt.Size)
74  	}
75  
76  	async := false
77  
78  	switch opt.Mode {
79  	case "sync":
80  		if opt.Size <= 0 {
81  			ci := fs.GetConfig(ctx)
82  			opt.Size = ci.Transfers
83  		}
84  		if opt.Timeout <= 0 {
85  			opt.Timeout = opt.DefaultTimeoutSync
86  		}
87  	case "async":
88  		if opt.Size <= 0 {
89  			opt.Size = opt.DefaultBatchSizeAsync
90  		}
91  		if opt.Timeout <= 0 {
92  			opt.Timeout = opt.DefaultTimeoutAsync
93  		}
94  		async = true
95  	case "off":
96  		opt.Size = 0
97  	default:
98  		return nil, fmt.Errorf("batcher: batch mode must be sync|async|off not %q", opt.Mode)
99  	}
100  
101  	b := &Batcher[Item, Result]{
102  		opt:    opt,
103  		f:      f,
104  		commit: commit,
105  		async:  async,
106  		in:     make(chan request[Item, Result], opt.Size),
107  		closed: make(chan struct{}),
108  	}
109  	if b.Batching() {
110  		b.atexit = atexit.Register(b.Shutdown)
111  		b.wg.Add(1)
112  		go b.commitLoop(context.Background())
