357  
358  func (f *futureImpl) Get(ctx Context, valuePtr interface{}) error {
359  	assertNotInReadOnlyState(ctx)
360  	more := f.channel.Receive(ctx, nil)
361  	if more {
362  		panic("not closed")
363  	}
364  	if !f.ready {
365  		panic("not ready")
366  	}
367  	if f.err != nil || f.value == nil || valuePtr == nil {
368  		return f.err
369  	}
370  	rf := reflect.ValueOf(valuePtr)
371  	if rf.Type().Kind() != reflect.Ptr {
372  		return errors.New("valuePtr parameter is not a pointer")
373  	}
374  
375  	if payload, ok := f.value.(*commonpb.Payloads); ok {
376  		if _, ok2 := valuePtr.(**commonpb.Payloads); !ok2 {
377  			if err := decodeArg(getDataConverterFromWorkflowContext(ctx), payload, valuePtr); err != nil {
378  				return err
379  			}
380  			return f.err
381  		}
382  	}
383  
384  	fv := reflect.ValueOf(f.value)
385  	// If the value set was a pointer and is the same type as the wanted result,
386  	// instead of panicking because it is not a pointer to a pointer, we will just
387  	// set the pointer
388  	if fv.Kind() == reflect.Ptr && fv.Type() == rf.Type() {
389  		rf.Elem().Set(fv.Elem())
390  	} else {
391  		rf.Elem().Set(fv)
392  	}
393  	return f.err
394  }
395  
396  // Used by selectorImpl
397  // If Future is ready returns its value immediately.
398  // If not registers callback which is called when it is ready.
399  func (f *futureImpl) GetAsync(callback *receiveCallback) (v interface{}, ok bool, err error) {
400  	_, _, more := f.channel.receiveAsyncImpl(callback)
401  	// Future uses Channel.Close to indicate that it is ready.
402  	// So more being true (channel is still open) indicates future is not ready.
403  	if more {
404  		return nil, false, nil
405  	}
406  	if !f.ready {
407  		panic("not ready")
408  	}
409  	return f.value, true, f.err
410  }
411  
412  // RemoveReceiveCallback removes the callback from future's channel to avoid closure leak.
413  // Used by selectorImpl
414  func (f *futureImpl) RemoveReceiveCallback(callback *receiveCallback) {
415  	f.channel.removeReceiveCallback(callback)
416  }
417  
418  func (f *futureImpl) IsReady() bool {
419  	return f.ready
420  }
421  
422  func (f *futureImpl) Set(value interface{}, err error) {
423  	if f.ready {
424  		panic("already set")
425  	}
426  	f.value = value
427  	f.err = err
428  	f.ready = true
429  	f.channel.Close()
430  	for _, ch := range f.chained {
431  		ch.Set(f.value, f.err)
432  	}
433  }
434  
435  func (f *futureImpl) SetValue(value interface{}) {
436  	if f.ready {
437  		panic("already set")
438  	}
439  	f.Set(value, nil)
440  }
441  
442  func (f *futureImpl) SetError(err error) {
443  	if f.ready {
444  		panic("already set")
445  	}
446  	f.Set(nil, err)
447  }
448  
449  func (f *futureImpl) Chain(future Future) {
450  	if f.ready {
451  		panic("already set")
452  	}
453  
454  	ch, ok := future.(asyncFuture)
455  	if !ok {
456  		panic("cannot chain Future that wasn't created with workflow.NewFuture")
457  	}
458  	if !ch.IsReady() {
459  		ch.ChainFuture(f)
460  		return
461  	}
462  	val, err := ch.GetValueAndError()
463  	f.Set(val, err)
464  }
465  
466  func (f *futureImpl) ChainFuture(future Future) {
467  	f.chained = append(f.chained, future.(asyncFuture))
468  }
469  
470  func (f *futureImpl) GetValueAndError() (interface{}, error) {
471  	return f.value, f.err
472  }
473  
474  func (f *childWorkflowFutureImpl) GetChildWorkflowExecution() Future {
475  	return f.executionFuture
476  }
477  
478  func (f *childWorkflowFutureImpl) SignalChildWorkflow(ctx Context, signalName string, data interface{}) Future {
479  	assertNotInReadOnlyState(ctx)
480  	var childExec WorkflowExecution
481  	if err := f.GetChildWorkflowExecution().Get(ctx, &childExec); err != nil {
482  		return f.GetChildWorkflowExecution()
483  	}
484  
485  	i := getWorkflowOutboundInterceptor(ctx)
486  	// Put header on context before executing
487  	ctx = workflowContextWithNewHeader(ctx)
488  	return i.SignalChildWorkflow(ctx, childExec.ID, signalName, data)
489  }
490  
491  func (f *nexusOperationFutureImpl) GetNexusOperationExecution() Future {
492  	return f.executionFuture
493  }
494  
495  func newWorkflowContext(
496  	env WorkflowEnvironment,
497  	interceptors []WorkerInterceptor,
498  ) (*workflowEnvironmentInterceptor, Context, error) {
499  	// Create context with default values
500  	ctx := WithValue(background, workflowEnvironmentContextKey, env)
501  	var resultPtr *workflowResult
502  	ctx = WithValue(ctx, workflowResultContextKey, &resultPtr)
503  	info := env.WorkflowInfo()
504  	ctx = WithWorkflowNamespace(ctx, info.Namespace)
505  	ctx = WithWorkflowTaskQueue(ctx, info.TaskQueueName)
506  	getWorkflowEnvOptions(ctx).WorkflowExecutionTimeout = info.WorkflowExecutionTimeout
507  	ctx = WithWorkflowRunTimeout(ctx, info.WorkflowRunTimeout)
508  	ctx = WithWorkflowTaskTimeout(ctx, info.WorkflowTaskTimeout)
509  	ctx = WithTaskQueue(ctx, info.TaskQueueName)
510  	ctx = WithDataConverter(ctx, env.GetDataConverter())
511  	ctx = withContextPropagators(ctx, env.GetContextPropagators())
512  	getActivityOptions(ctx).OriginalTaskQueueName = info.TaskQueueName
513  
514  	// Create interceptor and put it on context as inbound and put it on context
515  	// as the default outbound interceptor before init
516  	envInterceptor := &workflowEnvironmentInterceptor{env: env}
517  	envInterceptor.inboundInterceptor = envInterceptor
518  	envInterceptor.outboundInterceptor = envInterceptor
519  	ctx = WithValue(ctx, workflowEnvInterceptorContextKey, envInterceptor)
520  	ctx = WithValue(ctx, workflowInterceptorContextKey, envInterceptor.outboundInterceptor)
521  
522  	// Intercept, run init, and put the new outbound interceptor on the context
523  	for i := len(interceptors) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
524  		envInterceptor.inboundInterceptor = interceptors[i].InterceptWorkflow(ctx, envInterceptor.inboundInterceptor)
525  	}
526  	err := envInterceptor.inboundInterceptor.Init(envInterceptor)
527  	if err != nil {
528  		return nil, nil, err
529  	}
530  	ctx = WithValue(ctx, workflowInterceptorContextKey, envInterceptor.outboundInterceptor)
531  
532  	return envInterceptor, ctx, nil
533  }
534  
535  func (d *syncWorkflowDefinition) Execute(env WorkflowEnvironment, header *commonpb.Header, input *commonpb.Payloads) {
536  	envInterceptor, rootCtx, err := newWorkflowContext(env, env.GetRegistry().interceptors)
537  	if err != nil {
538  		panic(err)
539  	}
540  	dispatcher, rootCtx := newDispatcher(
541  		rootCtx,
542  		envInterceptor,
543  		func(ctx Context) {
544  			r := &workflowResult{}
545  
546  			// We want to execute the user workflow definition from the first workflow task started,
547  			// so they can see everything before that. Here we would have all initialization done, hence
548  			// we are yielding.
549  			state := getState(d.rootCtx)
550  			state.yield("yield before executing to setup state")
551  			state.unblocked()
552  
553  			r.workflowResult, r.error = d.workflow.Execute(d.rootCtx, input)
554  			rpp := getWorkflowResultPointerPointer(ctx)
555  			*rpp = r
556  		}, getWorkflowEnvironment(rootCtx).DrainUnhandledUpdates)
557  
558  	// set the information from the headers that is to be propagated in the workflow context
559  	rootCtx, err = workflowContextWithHeaderPropagated(rootCtx, header, env.GetContextPropagators())
560  	if err != nil {
561  		panic(err)
562  	}
563  
564  	d.rootCtx, d.cancel = WithCancel(rootCtx)
565  	d.dispatcher = dispatcher
566  	envInterceptor.dispatcher = dispatcher
567  
568  	getWorkflowEnvironment(d.rootCtx).RegisterCancelHandler(func() {
569  		// It is ok to call this method multiple times.
570  		// it doesn't do anything new, the context remains canceled.
571  		d.cancel()
572  	})
573  
574  	getWorkflowEnvironment(d.rootCtx).RegisterSignalHandler(
575  		func(name string, input *commonpb.Payloads, header *commonpb.Header) error {
576  			// Put the header on context
577  			rootCtx, err := workflowContextWithHeaderPropagated(d.rootCtx, header, env.GetContextPropagators())
578  			if err != nil {
579  				return err
580  			}
581  			return envInterceptor.inboundInterceptor.HandleSignal(rootCtx, &HandleSignalInput{SignalName: name, Arg: input})
582  		},
583  	)
584  
585  	getWorkflowEnvironment(d.rootCtx).RegisterUpdateHandler(
586  		func(name string, id string, serializedArgs *commonpb.Payloads, header *commonpb.Header, callbacks UpdateCallbacks) {
587  			defaultUpdateHandler(d.rootCtx, name, id, serializedArgs, header, callbacks, updateSchedulerImpl{d.dispatcher})
588  		})
589  
590  	getWorkflowEnvironment(d.rootCtx).RegisterQueryHandler(
591  		func(queryType string, queryArgs *commonpb.Payloads, header *commonpb.Header) (*commonpb.Payloads, error) {
592  			// Put the header on context if server supports it
593  			rootCtx, err := workflowContextWithHeaderPropagated(d.rootCtx, header, env.GetContextPropagators())
594  			if err != nil {
595  				return nil, err
596  			}
597  
598  			// As a special case, we handle __temporal_workflow_metadata query
599  			// here instead of in workflowExecutionEventHandlerImpl.ProcessQuery
600  			// because we need the context environment to do so.
601  			if queryType == QueryTypeWorkflowMetadata {
602  				if result, err := getWorkflowMetadata(rootCtx); err != nil {
603  					return nil, err
604  				} else {
605  					// Use raw value built from default converter because we don't want to use
606  					// user-conversion
607  					resultPayload, err := converter.GetDefaultDataConverter().ToPayload(result)
608  					if err != nil {
609  						return nil, err
610  					}
611  					return encodeArg(getDataConverterFromWorkflowContext(rootCtx), converter.NewRawValue(resultPayload))
612  				}
613  			}
614  
615  			eo := getWorkflowEnvOptions(rootCtx)
616  			// A handler must be present since it is needed for argument decoding,
617  			// even if the interceptor intercepts query handling
618  			handler, ok := eo.queryHandlers[queryType]
619  			if !ok {
620  				keys := []string{QueryTypeStackTrace, QueryTypeOpenSessions, QueryTypeWorkflowMetadata}
621  				for k := range eo.queryHandlers {
622  					keys = append(keys, k)
623  				}
624  				return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown queryType %v. KnownQueryTypes=%v", queryType, keys)
625  			}
626  
627  			// Decode the arguments
628  			args, err := decodeArgsToRawValues(handler.dataConverter, reflect.TypeOf(handler.fn), queryArgs)
629  			if err != nil {
630  				return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to decode the input for queryType: %v, with error: %w", handler.queryType, err)
631  			}
632  
633  			// Invoke
634  			result, err := envInterceptor.inboundInterceptor.HandleQuery(
635  				rootCtx,
636  				&HandleQueryInput{QueryType: queryType, Args: args},
637  			)
638  
639  			// Encode the result
640  			var serializedResult *commonpb.Payloads
641  			if err == nil {
642  				serializedResult, err = encodeArg(handler.dataConverter, result)
643  			}
644  			return serializedResult, err
645  		},
646  	)
647  }
648  
649  func (d *syncWorkflowDefinition) OnWorkflowTaskStarted(deadlockDetectionTimeout time.Duration) {
650  	executeDispatcher(d.rootCtx, d.dispatcher, deadlockDetectionTimeout)
651  }
652  
653  func (d *syncWorkflowDefinition) StackTrace() string {
654  	return d.dispatcher.StackTrace()
655  }
656  
657  func (d *syncWorkflowDefinition) Close() {
658  	if d.dispatcher != nil {
659  		d.dispatcher.Close()
660  	}
661  }
662  
663  // NewDispatcher creates a new Dispatcher instance with a root coroutine function.
664  // Context passed to the root function is child of the passed rootCtx.
665  // This way rootCtx can be used to pass values to the coroutine code.
666  func newDispatcher(rootCtx Context, interceptor *workflowEnvironmentInterceptor, root func(ctx Context), allBlockedCallback func() bool) (*dispatcherImpl, Context) {
667  	env := getWorkflowEnvironment(rootCtx)
668  
669  	result := &dispatcherImpl{
670  		interceptor:        interceptor.outboundInterceptor,
671  		logger:             env.GetLogger(),
672  		deadlockDetector:   newDeadlockDetector(),
673  		allBlockedCallback: allBlockedCallback,
674  	}
675  	interceptor.dispatcher = result
676  	ctxWithState := result.interceptor.Go(rootCtx, "root", root)
677  	return result, ctxWithState
678  }
679  
680  // executeDispatcher executed coroutines in the calling thread and calls workflow completion callbacks
681  // if root workflow function returned
682  func executeDispatcher(ctx Context, dispatcher dispatcher, timeout time.Duration) {
683  	env := getWorkflowEnvironment(ctx)
684  	panicErr := dispatcher.ExecuteUntilAllBlocked(timeout)
685  	if panicErr != nil {
686  		env.Complete(nil, panicErr)
687  		return
688  	}
689  
690  	rp := *getWorkflowResultPointerPointer(ctx)
691  	if rp == nil {
692  		// Result is not set, so workflow is still executing
693  		return
694  	}
695  
696  	weo := getWorkflowEnvOptions(ctx)
697  	us := weo.getUnhandledSignalNames()
698  	if len(us) > 0 {
699  		env.GetLogger().Warn("Workflow has unhandled signals", "SignalNames", us)
700  	}
701  	// Warn if there are any update handlers still running
702  	type warnUpdate struct {
703  		Name string `json:"name"`
704  		ID   string `json:"id"`
705  	}
706  	var updatesToWarn []warnUpdate
707  	for _, info := range weo.getRunningUpdateHandles() {
708  		if weo.updateHandlers[info.Name].unfinishedPolicy == HandlerUnfinishedPolicyWarnAndAbandon {
709  			updatesToWarn = append(updatesToWarn, warnUpdate{
710  				Name: info.Name,
711  				ID:   info.ID,
712  			})
713  		}
714  	}
715  
716  	// Verify that the workflow did not fail. If it did we will not warn about unhandled updates.
717  	var canceledErr *CanceledError
718  	var contErr *ContinueAsNewError
719  	if len(updatesToWarn) > 0 && (rp.error == nil || errors.As(rp.error, &canceledErr) || errors.As(rp.error, &contErr)) {
720  		env.GetLogger().Warn(unhandledUpdateWarningMessage, "Updates", updatesToWarn)
721  	}
722  
723  	env.Complete(rp.workflowResult, rp.error)
724  }
725  
726  // For troubleshooting stack pretty printing only.
727  // Set to true to see full stack trace that includes framework methods.
728  const disableCleanStackTraces = false
729  
730  func getState(ctx Context) *coroutineState {
731  	s := ctx.Value(coroutinesContextKey)
732  	if s == nil {
733  		panic("getState: not workflow context")
734  	}
735  	state := s.(*coroutineState)
736  	if !state.dispatcher.IsExecuting() {
737  		panic(panicIllegalAccessCoroutineState)
738  	}
739  	return state
740  }
741  
742  func assertNotInReadOnlyState(ctx Context) {
743  	state := getState(ctx)
744  	// use the dispatcher state instead of the coroutine state because contexts can be
745  	// shared
746  	if state.dispatcher.getIsReadOnly() {
747  		panic(panicIllegalAccessCoroutineState)
748  	}
749  }
750  
751  func assertNotInReadOnlyStateCancellation(ctx Context) {
752  	s := ctx.Value(coroutinesContextKey)
753  	if s == nil {
754  		panic("assertNotInReadOnlyStateCtxCancellation: not workflow context")
755  	}
756  	state := s.(*coroutineState)
757  	// For cancellation the dispatcher may not be running because workflow cancellation
758  	// is sent outside of the dispatchers loop.
759  	if state.dispatcher.IsClosed() {
760  		panic(panicIllegalAccessCoroutineState)
761  	}
762  	// use the dispatcher state instead of the coroutine state because contexts can be
763  	// shared
764  	if state.dispatcher.getIsReadOnly() {
765  		panic(panicIllegalAccessCoroutineState)
766  	}
767  }
768  
769  func getStateIfRunning(ctx Context) *coroutineState {
770  	if ctx == nil {
771  		return nil
772  	}
773  	s := ctx.Value(coroutinesContextKey)
774  	if s == nil {
775  		return nil
776  	}
777  	state := s.(*coroutineState)
778  	if !state.dispatcher.IsExecuting() {
779  		return nil
780  	}
781  	return state
782  }
783  
784  func (c *channelImpl) Name() string {
785  	return c.name
786  }
787  
788  func (c *channelImpl) CanReceiveWithoutBlocking() bool {
789  	return c.recValue != nil || len(c.buffer) > 0 || len(c.blockedSends) > 0 || c.closed
790  }
791  
792  func (c *channelImpl) CanSendWithoutBlocking() bool {
793  	return len(c.buffer) < c.size || len(c.blockedReceives) > 0
794  }
795  
796  func (c *channelImpl) Receive(ctx Context, valuePtr interface{}) (more bool) {
797  	assertNotInReadOnlyState(ctx)
798  	state := getState(ctx)
799  	hasResult := false
800  	var result interface{}
801  	callback := &receiveCallback{
802  		fn: func(v interface{}, m bool) bool {
803  			result = v
804  			hasResult = true
805  			more = m
806  			return true
807  		},
808  	}
809  
810  	for {
811  		hasResult = false
812  		v, ok, m := c.receiveAsyncImpl(callback)
813  
814  		if !ok && !m { // channel closed and empty
815  			return m
816  		}
817  
818  		if ok || !m {
819  			err := c.assignValue(v, valuePtr)
820  			if err == nil {
821  				state.unblocked()
822  				return m
823  			}
824  			continue // corrupt signal. Drop and reset process
825  		}
826  		for {
827  			if hasResult {
828  				err := c.assignValue(result, valuePtr)
829  				if err == nil {
830  					state.unblocked()
831  					return more
832  				}
833  				break // Corrupt signal. Drop and reset process.
834  			}
835  			state.yield("blocked on " + c.name + ".Receive")
836  		}
837  	}
838  
839  }
840  
841  func (c *channelImpl) ReceiveWithTimeout(ctx Context, timeout time.Duration, valuePtr interface{}) (ok, more bool) {
842  	okAwait, err := AwaitWithTimeout(ctx, timeout, func() bool { return c.Len() > 0 })
843  	if err != nil { // context canceled
844  		return false, true
845  	}
846  	if !okAwait { // timed out
847  		return false, true
848  	}
849  	ok, more = c.ReceiveAsyncWithMoreFlag(valuePtr)
850  	if !ok {
851  		panic("unexpected empty channel")
852  	}
853  	return true, more
854  }
855  
856  func (c *channelImpl) ReceiveAsync(valuePtr interface{}) (ok bool) {
857  	ok, _ = c.ReceiveAsyncWithMoreFlag(valuePtr)
858  	return ok
859  }
860  
861  func (c *channelImpl) ReceiveAsyncWithMoreFlag(valuePtr interface{}) (ok bool, more bool) {
862  	for {
863  		v, ok, more := c.receiveAsyncImpl(nil)
864  		if !ok && !more { // channel closed and empty
865  			return ok, more
866  		}
867  
868  		err := c.assignValue(v, valuePtr)
869  		if err != nil {
870  			continue
871  			// keep consuming until a good signal is hit or channel is drained
872  		}
873  		return ok, more
874  	}
875  }
876  
877  func (c *channelImpl) Len() int {
878  	result := len(c.buffer) + len(c.blockedSends)
879  	if c.recValue != nil {
880  		result = result + 1
881  	}
882  	return result
883  }
884  
885  // ok = true means that value was received
886  // more = true means that channel is not closed and more deliveries are possible
887  func (c *channelImpl) receiveAsyncImpl(callback *receiveCallback) (v interface{}, ok bool, more bool) {
888  	if c.recValue != nil {
889  		r := *c.recValue
890  		c.recValue = nil
891  		return r, true, true
892  	}
893  	if len(c.buffer) > 0 {
894  		r := c.buffer[0]
895  		c.buffer[0] = nil
896  		c.buffer = c.buffer[1:]
897  
898  		// Move blocked sends into buffer
899  		for len(c.blockedSends) > 0 {
900  			b := c.blockedSends[0]
901  			c.blockedSends[0] = nil
902  			c.blockedSends = c.blockedSends[1:]
903  			if b.fn() {
904  				c.buffer = append(c.buffer, b.value)
905  				break
906  			}
907  		}
908  
909  		return r, true, true
910  	}
911  	if c.closed {
912  		return nil, false, false
913  	}
914  	for len(c.blockedSends) > 0 {
915  		b := c.blockedSends[0]
916  		c.blockedSends[0] = nil
917  		c.blockedSends = c.blockedSends[1:]
918  		if b.fn() {
919  			return b.value, true, true
920  		}
921  	}
922  	if callback != nil {
923  		c.blockedReceives = append(c.blockedReceives, callback)
924  	}
925  	return nil, false, true
926  }
927  
928  func (c *channelImpl) removeReceiveCallback(callback *receiveCallback) {
929  	for i, blockedCallback := range c.blockedReceives {
930  		if callback == blockedCallback {
931  			c.blockedReceives = append(c.blockedReceives[:i], c.blockedReceives[i+1:]...)
932  			break
933  		}
934  	}
935  }
936  
937  func (c *channelImpl) removeSendCallback(callback *sendCallback) {
938  	for i, blockedCallback := range c.blockedSends {
939  		if callback == blockedCallback {
940  			c.blockedSends = append(c.blockedSends[:i], c.blockedSends[i+1:]...)
941  			break
942  		}
943  	}
944  }
945  
946  func (c *channelImpl) Send(ctx Context, v interface{}) {
947  	state := getState(ctx)
948  	valueConsumed := false
949  	callback := &sendCallback{
950  		value: v,
951  		fn: func() bool {
952  			valueConsumed = true
953  			return true
954  		},
955  	}
956  	ok := c.sendAsyncImpl(v, callback)
957  	if ok {
958  		state.unblocked()
959  		return
960  	}
961  	for {
962  		if valueConsumed {
963  			state.unblocked()
964  			return
965  		}
966  
967  		// Check for closed in the loop as close can be called when send is blocked
968  		if c.closed {
969  			panic("Closed channel")
970  		}
971  		state.yield("blocked on " + c.name + ".Send")
972  	}
973  }
974  
975  func (c *channelImpl) SendAsync(v interface{}) (ok bool) {
976  	return c.sendAsyncImpl(v, nil)
977  }
978  
979  func (c *channelImpl) sendAsyncImpl(v interface{}, pair *sendCallback) (ok bool) {
980  	if c.closed {
981  		panic("Closed channel")
982  	}
983  	for len(c.blockedReceives) > 0 {
984  		blockedGet := c.blockedReceives[0].fn
985  		c.blockedReceives[0] = nil
986  		c.blockedReceives = c.blockedReceives[1:]
987  		// false from callback indicates that value wasn't consumed
988  		if blockedGet(v, true) {
989  			return true
990  		}
991  	}
992  	if len(c.buffer) < c.size {
993  		c.buffer = append(c.buffer, v)
994  		return true
995  	}
996  	if pair != nil {
997  		c.blockedSends = append(c.blockedSends, pair)
998  	}
999  	return false
1000  }
1001  
1002  func (c *channelImpl) Close() {
1003  	c.closed = true
1004  	// Use a copy of blockedReceives for iteration as invoking callback could result in modification
1005  	copy := append(c.blockedReceives[:0:0], c.blockedReceives...)
1006  	for _, callback := range copy {
1007  		callback.fn(nil, false)
1008  	}
1009  	// All blocked sends are going to panic
1010  }
1011  
1012  // Takes a value and assigns that 'to' value. logs a metric if it is unable to deserialize
1013  func (c *channelImpl) assignValue(from interface{}, to interface{}) error {
1014  	err := decodeAndAssignValue(c.dataConverter, from, to)
1015  	// add to metrics
1016  	if err != nil {
1017  		c.env.GetLogger().Error(fmt.Sprintf("Deserialization error. Corrupted signal received on channel %s.", c.name), tagError, err)
1018  		c.env.GetMetricsHandler().Counter(metrics.CorruptedSignalsCounter).Inc(1)
1019  	}
1020  	return err
1021  }
1022  
1023  // initialYield is called at the beginning of coroutine execution.
1024  // stackDepth is the depth of the top of the stack to omit when a stack trace is generated,
1025  // to hide frames internal to the framework.
1026  func (s *coroutineState) initialYield(stackDepth int, status string) {
1027  	if s.blocked.Swap(true) {
1028  		panic("trying to block on coroutine which is already blocked, most likely a wrong Context is used to do blocking" +
1029  			" call (like Future.Get() or Channel.Receive()")
1030  	}
1031  	keepBlocked := true
1032  	for keepBlocked {
1033  		f := <-s.unblock
1034  		keepBlocked = f(status, stackDepth+1)
1035  	}
1036  	s.blocked.Swap(false)
1037  }
1038  
1039  // isPanicking reports whether the current goroutine is executing during panic unwinding. It checks
1040  // for runtime.gopanic on the call stack via runtime.Callers().
1041  func isPanicking() bool {
1042  	var pcs [20]uintptr
1043  	n := runtime.Callers(1, pcs[:])
1044  	frames := runtime.CallersFrames(pcs[:n])
1045  	for {
1046  		frame, more := frames.Next()
1047  		if frame.Function == "runtime.gopanic" {
1048  			return true
1049  		}
1050  		if !more {
1051  			break
1052  		}
1053  	}
1054  	return false
1055  }
1056  
1057  // yield indicates that coroutine cannot make progress and should sleep
1058  // this call blocks
1059  func (s *coroutineState) yield(status string) {
1060  	if isPanicking() {
1061  		// Unfortunately we lose the real panic message here, but the stack trace will still contain
1062  		// the right lines.
1063  		panic(errors.New(
1064  			"yield during panic unwinding: a deferred function attempted to block " +
1065  				"the coroutine while a panic was in progress"))
1066  	}
1067  	s.aboutToBlock <- true
1068  	s.initialYield(3, status) // omit three levels of stack. To adjust change to 0 and count the lines to remove.
1069  	s.keptBlocked = true
1070  }
1071  
1072  func getStackTrace(coroutineName, status string, stackDepth int) string {
1073  	top := fmt.Sprintf("coroutine %s [%s]:", coroutineName, status)
1074  	// Omit top stackDepth frames + top status line.
1075  	// Omit bottom two frames which is wrapping of coroutine in a goroutine.
1076  	return getStackTraceRaw(top, stackDepth*2+1, 4)
1077  }
1078  
1079  func getStackTraceRaw(top string, omitTop, omitBottom int) string {
1080  	stack := stackBuf[:runtime.Stack(stackBuf[:], false)]
1081  	outStack := filterStackTrace(string(stack), omitTop, omitBottom)
1082  	return strings.Join([]string{top, outStack}, "\n")
1083  }
1084  
1085  func filterStackTrace(stack string, omitTop, omitBottom int) string {
1086  	stack = strings.TrimRightFunc(stack, unicode.IsSpace)
1087  	if disableCleanStackTraces {
1088  		return stack
1089  	}
1090  
1091  	lines := strings.Split(stack, "\n")
1092  	omitEnd := len(lines) - omitBottom
1093  	// If the start is after the end, the depth was invalid originally so return
1094  	// the entire raw stack
1095  	if omitTop > omitEnd {
1096  		return stack
1097  	}
1098  	return strings.Join(lines[omitTop:omitEnd], "\n")
1099  }
1100  
1101  func getCoroStackTrace(crt *coroutineState, status string, stackDepth int) (string, error) {
1102  	// Can't dump goroutines selectively :(
1103  	// Instead, we identify a coroutine's stack trace by the *coroutineState pointer address
1104  	// in its function arguments. To avoid false positives, we also match on the fixed
1105  	// member function name.
1106  	stacks := stackBuf[:runtime.Stack(stackBuf[:], true)]
1107  	needle := []byte(fmt.Sprintf("/internal.(*coroutineState).run(%p,", crt))
1108  	idx := bytes.Index(stacks, needle)
1109  	if idx == -1 {
1110  		if len(stacks) == len(stackBuf) {
1111  			return "", fmt.Errorf("coroutine not found: %w", errStackTraceTruncated)
1112  		}
1113  		// NOTE: This could happen if coroutineState is moved between runtime.Stack(...)
1114  		// and formatting needle. However, Go's GC is currently non-moving.
1115  		return "", errCoroStackNotFound
1116  	}
1117  
1118  	// coroStack spans from the stackDelim before idx to the stackDelim after idx
1119  	stackDelim := []byte("\n\n")
1120  	coroStack := stacks
1121  	if start := bytes.LastIndex(stacks[:idx], stackDelim); start != -1 {
1122  		start += len(stackDelim) // skip over delimiter
1123  		coroStack = stacks[start:]
1124  	}
1125  	coroStack, _, _ = bytes.Cut(coroStack, stackDelim)
1126  
1127  	// Omit top stackDepth frames + top status line.
1128  	// Omit bottom two frames which is wrapping of coroutine in a goroutine.
1129  	outStack := filterStackTrace(string(coroStack), stackDepth*2+1, 4)
1130  	return fmt.Sprintf("coroutine %s [%s]:\n%s", crt.name, status, outStack), nil
1131  }
1132  
1133  // unblocked is called by coroutine to indicate that since the last time yield was unblocked channel or select
1134  // where unblocked versus calling yield again after checking their condition
1135  func (s *coroutineState) unblocked() {
1136  	s.keptBlocked = false
1137  }
1138  
1139  func (s *coroutineState) call(timeout time.Duration) {
1140  	s.unblock <- func(status string, stackDepth int) bool {
1141  		return false // unblock
1142  	}
1143  
1144  	// Defaults are populated in the worker options during worker startup, but test environment
1145  	// may have no default value for the deadlock detection timeout, so we also need to set it here for
1146  	// backwards compatibility.
1147  	if timeout == 0 {
1148  		timeout = defaultDeadlockDetectionTimeout
1149  		if debugMode {
1150  			timeout = unlimitedDeadlockDetectionTimeout
1151  		}
1152  	}
1153  	deadlockTicker := s.dispatcher.deadlockDetector.begin(timeout)
1154  	defer deadlockTicker.end()
1155  
1156  	select {
1157  	case <-s.aboutToBlock:
1158  	case <-deadlockTicker.reached():
1159  		// Use workflowPanicError since this used to call panic(msg)
1160  		st, err := getCoroStackTrace(s, "running", 0)
1161  		if err != nil {
1162  			st = fmt.Sprintf("<%s>", err)
1163  		}
1164  		msg := fmt.Sprintf("[TMPRL1101] Potential deadlock detected: "+
1165  			"workflow goroutine %q didn't yield for over a second", s.name)
1166  		s.closed.Store(true)
1167  		s.panicError = newWorkflowPanicError(msg, st)
1168  	}
1169  }
1170  
1171  func (s *coroutineState) close() {
1172  	s.closed.Store(true)
1173  	s.aboutToBlock <- true
1174  }
1175  
1176  // exit tries to run Goexit on the coroutine and wait for it to exit
1177  // within timeout. If it doesn't exit within timeout, it will log a warning.
1178  func (s *coroutineState) exit(logger log.Logger, warnTimeout time.Duration) {
1179  	if !s.closed.Load() {
1180  		s.unblock <- func(status string, stackDepth int) bool {
1181  			runtime.Goexit()
1182  			return true
1183  		}
1184  
1185  		timer := time.NewTimer(warnTimeout)
1186  		defer timer.Stop()
1187  
1188  		select {
1189  		case <-s.aboutToBlock:
1190  			return
1191  		case <-timer.C:
1192  			st, err := getCoroStackTrace(s, "running", 0)
1193  			if err != nil {
1194  				st = fmt.Sprintf("<%s>", err)
1195  			}
1196  
1197  			logger.Warn(fmt.Sprintf("Workflow coroutine %q didn't exit within %v", s.name, warnTimeout), "stackTrace", st)
1198  		}
1199  		// We need to make sure the coroutine is closed, otherwise we risk concurrent coroutines running
1200  		// at the same time causing a race condition.
1201  		<-s.aboutToBlock
1202  	}
1203  }
1204  
1205  func (s *coroutineState) stackTrace() string {
1206  	if s.closed.Load() {
1207  		return ""
1208  	}
1209  	stackCh := make(chan string, 1)
1210  	s.unblock <- func(status string, stackDepth int) bool {
1211  		stackCh <- getStackTrace(s.name, status, stackDepth+2)
1212  		return true
1213  	}
1214  	return <-stackCh
1215  }
1216  
1217  func (s *coroutineState) run(ctx Context, f func(ctx Context)) {
1218  	defer runtime.KeepAlive(&s) // keep receiver argument alive for getCoroStackTrace
1219  	defer s.close()
1220  	defer func() {
1221  		if r := recover(); r != nil {
1222  			st := getStackTrace(s.name, "panic", 4)
1223  			s.panicError = newWorkflowPanicError(r, st)
1224  		}
1225  	}()
1226  	s.initialYield(1, "")
1227  	f(ctx)
1228  }
1229  
1230  func (d *dispatcherImpl) NewCoroutine(ctx Context, name string, highPriority bool, f func(ctx Context)) Context {
1231  	if name == "" {
1232  		name = fmt.Sprintf("%v", d.sequence+1)
1233  	}
1234  	state := d.newState(name, highPriority)
1235  	spawned := WithValue(ctx, coroutinesContextKey, state)
1236  	go state.run(spawned, f)
1237  	return spawned
1238  }
1239  
1240  func (d *dispatcherImpl) newState(name string, highPriority bool) *coroutineState {
1241  	c := &coroutineState{
1242  		name:         name,
1243  		dispatcher:   d,
1244  		aboutToBlock: make(chan bool, 1),
1245  		unblock:      make(chan unblockFunc),
1246  	}
1247  	d.sequence++
1248  	if highPriority {
1249  		// Update requests need to be added to the front of the dispatchers coroutine list so they
1250  		// are handled before the root coroutine.
1251  		d.newEagerCoroutines = append(d.newEagerCoroutines, c)
1252  	} else {
1253  		d.coroutines = append(d.coroutines, c)
1254  	}
1255  	return c
1256  }
1257  
1258  func (d *dispatcherImpl) IsClosed() bool {
1259  	d.mutex.Lock()
1260  	defer d.mutex.Unlock()
1261  	return d.closed
1262  }
1263  
1264  func (d *dispatcherImpl) ExecuteUntilAllBlocked(deadlockDetectionTimeout time.Duration) (err error) {
1265  	d.mutex.Lock()
1266  	if d.closed {
1267  		d.mutex.Unlock()
1268  		panic("dispatcher is closed")
1269  	}
1270  	if d.executing {
1271  		d.mutex.Unlock()
1272  		panic("call to ExecuteUntilAllBlocked (possibly from a coroutine) while it is already running")
1273  	}
1274  	d.executing = true
1275  	d.mutex.Unlock()
1276  	defer func() {
1277  		d.mutex.Lock()
1278  		d.executing = false
1279  		d.mutex.Unlock()
1280  	}()
1281  	allBlocked := false
1282  	// Keep executing until at least one goroutine made some progress
1283  	for !allBlocked || d.allBlockedCallback() {
1284  		d.coroutines = append(d.newEagerCoroutines, d.coroutines...)
1285  		d.newEagerCoroutines = nil
1286  		// Give every coroutine chance to execute removing closed ones
1287  		allBlocked = true
1288  		lastSequence := d.sequence
1289  		for i := 0; i < len(d.coroutines); i++ {
1290  			c := d.coroutines[i]
1291  			if !c.closed.Load() {
1292  				// TODO: Support handling of panic in a coroutine by dispatcher.
1293  				// TODO: Dump all outstanding coroutines if one of them panics
1294  				c.call(deadlockDetectionTimeout)
1295  			}
1296  			// c.call() can close the context so check again
1297  			if c.closed.Load() {
1298  				// remove the closed one from the slice
1299  				d.coroutines = append(d.coroutines[:i],
1300  					d.coroutines[i+1:]...)
1301  				i--
1302  				if c.panicError != nil {
1303  					return c.panicError
1304  				}
1305  				allBlocked = false
1306  
1307  			} else {
1308  				allBlocked = allBlocked && (c.keptBlocked || c.closed.Load())
1309  			}
1310  			// If any eager coroutines were created by the last coroutine we
1311  			// need to schedule them now.
1312  			if len(d.newEagerCoroutines) > 0 {
1313  				d.coroutines = slices.Insert(d.coroutines, i+1, d.newEagerCoroutines...)
1314  				d.newEagerCoroutines = nil
1315  				allBlocked = false
1316  			}
1317  		}
1318  		// Set allBlocked to false if new coroutines where created
1319  		allBlocked = allBlocked && lastSequence == d.sequence
1320  	}
1321  	return nil
1322  }
1323  
1324  func (d *dispatcherImpl) IsDone() bool {
1325  	d.mutex.Lock()
1326  	defer d.mutex.Unlock()
1327  	return len(d.coroutines) == 0
1328  }
1329  
1330  func (d *dispatcherImpl) IsExecuting() bool {
1331  	d.mutex.Lock()
1332  	defer d.mutex.Unlock()
1333  	return d.executing
1334  }
1335  
1336  func (d *dispatcherImpl) getIsReadOnly() bool {
1337  	d.mutex.Lock()
1338  	defer d.mutex.Unlock()
1339  	return d.readOnly
1340  }
1341  
1342  func (d *dispatcherImpl) setIsReadOnly(readOnly bool) {
1343  	d.mutex.Lock()
1344  	defer d.mutex.Unlock()
1345  	d.readOnly = readOnly
1346  }
1347  
1348  func (d *dispatcherImpl) Close() {
1349  	d.mutex.Lock()
1350  	if d.closed {
1351  		d.mutex.Unlock()
1352  		return
1353  	}
1354  	d.closed = true
1355  	d.mutex.Unlock()
1356  	// We need to exit the coroutines in a separate goroutine because:
1357  	// 	* The coroutine may be stuck and won't respond to the exit request.
1358  	// 	* On exit the coroutines defers will still run and that may block.
1359  	go func() {
1360  		for _, c := range d.coroutines {
1361  			c.exit(d.logger, defaultDeadlockDetectionTimeout)
1362  		}
1363  	}()
1364  }
1365  
1366  func (d *dispatcherImpl) StackTrace() string {
1367  	var result string
1368  	for i := 0; i < len(d.coroutines); i++ {
1369  		c := d.coroutines[i]
1370  		if !c.closed.Load() {
1371  			if len(result) > 0 {
1372  				result += "\n\n"
1373  			}
1374  			result += c.stackTrace()
1375  		}
1376  	}
1377  	return result
1378  }
1379  
1380  func (s *selectorImpl) AddReceive(c ReceiveChannel, f func(c ReceiveChannel, more bool)) Selector {
1381  	s.cases = append(s.cases, &selectCase{channel: c.(*channelImpl), receiveFunc: &f})
1382  	return s
1383  }
1384  
1385  func (s *selectorImpl) AddSend(c SendChannel, v interface{}, f func()) Selector {
1386  	s.cases = append(s.cases, &selectCase{channel: c.(*channelImpl), sendFunc: &f, sendValue: &v})
1387  	return s
1388  }
1389  
1390  func (s *selectorImpl) AddFuture(future Future, f func(future Future)) Selector {
1391  	asyncF, ok := future.(asyncFuture)
1392  	if !ok {
1393  		panic("cannot chain Future that wasn't created with workflow.NewFuture")
1394  	}
1395  	s.cases = append(s.cases, &selectCase{future: asyncF, futureFunc: &f})
1396  	return s
1397  }
1398  
1399  func (s *selectorImpl) AddDefault(f func()) {
1400  	s.defaultFunc = &f
1401  }
1402  
1403  func (s *selectorImpl) HasPending() bool {
1404  	for _, pair := range s.cases {
1405  		if pair.receiveFunc != nil && pair.channel.CanReceiveWithoutBlocking() {
1406  			return true
1407  		} else if pair.sendFunc != nil && pair.channel.CanSendWithoutBlocking() {
1408  			return true
1409  		} else if pair.futureFunc != nil && pair.future.IsReady() {
1410  			return true
1411  		}
1412  	}
1413  	return false
1414  }
1415  
1416  func (s *selectorImpl) Select(ctx Context) {
1417  	assertNotInReadOnlyState(ctx)
1418  	state := getState(ctx)
1419  	var readyBranch func()
1420  	var cleanups []func()
1421  	defer func() {
1422  		for _, c := range cleanups {
1423  			c()
1424  		}
1425  	}()
1426  
1427  	for _, pair := range s.cases {
1428  		if pair.receiveFunc != nil {
1429  			f := *pair.receiveFunc
1430  			c := pair.channel
1431  			hasDefault := s.defaultFunc != nil
1432  			callback := &receiveCallback{
1433  				fn: func(v interface{}, more bool) bool {
1434  					if readyBranch != nil {
1435  						return false
1436  					}
1437  					env := getWorkflowEnvironment(ctx)
1438  					dropSignalFlag := env.TryUse(SDKFlagBlockedSelectorSignalReceive)
1439  					channelLostMsgFlag := env.TryUse(SDKFlagWorkflowNewChannelLostMessages)
1440  
1441  					// Pre-store c.recValue to prevent signal loss when AddDefault
1442  					// blocks. Without channelLostMsgFlag, always pre-store (original
1443  					// #1624 fix). With channelLostMsgFlag, only pre-store when a
1444  					// default branch exists to avoid overwriting c.recValue when
1445  					// multiple selectors are blocked on the same channel.
1446  					storeNow := dropSignalFlag && (!channelLostMsgFlag || hasDefault)
1447  					if storeNow {
1448  						c.recValue = &v
1449  					}
1450  
1451  					readyBranch = func() {
1452  						if !storeNow {
1453  							c.recValue = &v
1454  						}
1455  						f(c, more)
1456  					}
1457  					return true
1458  				},
1459  			}
1460  			v, ok, more := c.receiveAsyncImpl(callback)
1461  			if ok || !more {
1462  				// Select() returns in this case/branch. The callback won't be called for this case. However, callback
1463  				// will be called for previous cases/branches. We should set readyBranch so that when other case/branch
1464  				// become ready they won't consume the value for this Select() call.
1465  				readyBranch = func() {
1466  				}
1467  				// Avoid assigning pointer to nil interface which makes
1468  				// c.RecValue != nil and breaks the nil check at the beginning of receiveAsyncImpl
1469  				if more {
1470  					c.recValue = &v
1471  				} else {
1472  					pair.receiveFunc = nil
1473  				}
1474  				f(c, more)
1475  				return
1476  			}
1477  			// callback closure is added to channel's blockedReceives, we need to clean it up to avoid closure leak
1478  			cleanups = append(cleanups, func() {
1479  				c.removeReceiveCallback(callback)
1480  			})
1481  		} else if pair.sendFunc != nil {
1482  			f := *pair.sendFunc
1483  			c := pair.channel
1484  			callback := &sendCallback{
1485  				value: *pair.sendValue,
1486  				fn: func() bool {
1487  					if readyBranch != nil {
1488  						return false
1489  					}
1490  					readyBranch = func() {
1491  						f()
1492  					}
1493  					return true
1494  				},
1495  			}
1496  			ok := c.sendAsyncImpl(*pair.sendValue, callback)
1497  			if ok {
1498  				// Select() returns in this case/branch. The callback won't be called for this case. However, callback
1499  				// will be called for previous cases/branches. We should set readyBranch so that when other case/branch
1500  				// become ready they won't consume the value for this Select() call.
1501  				readyBranch = func() {
1502  				}
1503  				f()
1504  				return
1505  			}
1506  			// callback closure is added to channel's blockedSends, we need to clean it up to avoid closure leak
1507  			cleanups = append(cleanups, func() {
1508  				c.removeSendCallback(callback)
1509  			})
1510  		} else if pair.futureFunc != nil {
1511  			p := pair
1512  			f := *p.futureFunc
1513  			callback := &receiveCallback{
1514  				fn: func(v interface{}, more bool) bool {
1515  					if readyBranch != nil {
1516  						return false
1517  					}
1518  					readyBranch = func() {
1519  						p.futureFunc = nil
1520  						f(p.future)
1521  					}
1522  					return true
1523  				},
1524  			}
1525  
1526  			_, ok, _ := p.future.GetAsync(callback)
1527  			if ok {
1528  				// Select() returns in this case/branch. The callback won't be called for this case. However, callback
1529  				// will be called for previous cases/branches. We should set readyBranch so that when other case/branch
1530  				// become ready they won't consume the value for this Select() call.
1531  				readyBranch = func() {
1532  				}
1533  				p.futureFunc = nil
1534  				f(p.future)
1535  				return
1536  			}
1537  			// callback closure is added to future's channel's blockedReceives, need to clean up to avoid leak
1538  			cleanups = append(cleanups, func() {
1539  				p.future.RemoveReceiveCallback(callback)
1540  			})
1541  		}
1542  	}
1543  	if s.defaultFunc != nil {
1544  		f := *s.defaultFunc
1545  		f()
1546  		return
1547  	}
1548  	for {
1549  		if readyBranch != nil {
1550  			readyBranch()
1551  			state.unblocked()
1552  			return
1553  		}
1554  		state.yield("blocked on " + s.name + ".Select")
1555  	}
1556  }

1000  					case laRetry := <-workflowTask.laRetryCh:
1001  						eventHandler := workflowContext.getEventHandler()

1006  						}

1009  							break processWorkflowLoop
1010  						}
1011  
1012  						laRetry.attempt++
1013  
1014  						if !wth.laTunnel.sendTask(laRetry) {
1015  							laRetry.attempt--
1016  						}

872  // DeterministicKeysFunc returns the keys of a map in a deterministic (sorted) order.
873  // cmp(a, b) should return a negative number when a < b, a positive number when
874  // a > b and zero when a == b. Keys are sorted by cmp.
875  // To be used in for loops in workflows for deterministic iteration.
876  func DeterministicKeysFunc[K comparable, V any](m map[K]V, cmp func(K, K) int) []K {
877  	return internal.DeterministicKeysFunc(m, cmp)
878  }

// sdk-go / internal/internal_workflow.go:1153-1168
1153  	deadlockTicker := s.dispatcher.deadlockDetector.begin(timeout)
1154  	defer deadlockTicker.end()
1155  
1156  	select {
1157  	case <-s.aboutToBlock:
1158  	case <-deadlockTicker.reached():
1159  		// Use workflowPanicError since this used to call panic(msg)
1160  		st, err := getCoroStackTrace(s, "running", 0)
1161  		if err != nil {
1162  			st = fmt.Sprintf("<%s>", err)
1163  		}
1164  		msg := fmt.Sprintf("[TMPRL1101] Potential deadlock detected: "+
1165  			"workflow goroutine %q didn't yield for over a second", s.name)
1166  		s.closed.Store(true)
1167  		s.panicError = newWorkflowPanicError(msg, st)
1168  	}
